### Athanay — 2014-10-13T17:47:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-newbie-in-need.11881/post-143728>

Hi Caroline,

Thanks for the warm welcome!

Why do you think non-native EMF is a major issue for me?

I do drink R/O water and have for the past year or two, I sometimes drink mineral water as well.
I don't have the book and am trying to get caught up on the podcasts.
I will create a journal, and tried to post a pic but couldn't figure out how. When I click on the blank picture it says there is an error.

I work from home most of the time, so I spend a majority of my day in my room. It's on the second floor of a house built in the last 5-7 years. I've been living here for over a year but am moving out at the end of October. There are several outlets in the room (7 total), two of which are right next to my bed - I try to not plug anything into those. My workstation has a lot going on (desktop computer with large monitor, large speakers, and various audio equipment) I have a surge protector with 6 outlets, all of which are used, and the two wall outlets are on either side of me, both in full-time use. I spend a lot of time in front of the computer, as both my work and hobbies involve audio editing/recording, which require a computer. The wireless router and modem used to be in the room below me, but are now across the hall. The roommate below me has a very similar setup to me.

The house is nice, but that obviously doesn't mean the electricity was set up well.

I don't have a smart phone, btw, and I now turn my cell to airplane mode at night. I try and get to bed between 10-11, but have had a lot of trouble falling asleep lately. I'm going to really make an effort to get to sleep earlier, and wake up earlier as well.

One day a week I work at my bosses house. He lives in the country, but the room we work in has the wireless modem/router, a cordless phone and a couple computers. I almost always feel worse after working at his place for a few hours.

The house I lived in when I started feeling bad (3+ years ago) was much older and probably had the dirtiest of electricity. The router was in my room for 2-3 years and the there was some sort of electric component for the house right outside my room. It turned out to have some issues that required it to be completely replaced according to the city standards. Not sure if this played a role in my initial collapse, but it seems likely after reading up on emf.

I live in Asheville, NC - which is a beautiful and clean mountain town. I'm not sure about cell phone towers in my area, but am trying to find out. There are power lines running all through the neighborhood, and they converge at a spot that is level with, and not too far from (40 yards +/-) my bedroom.

I should note that I didn't spend quite so much time in front of the computer until I started my current job, roughly a year ago.

My family history has me on a pretty broad spectrum. My Mom's side is more type-A, and has had heart disease, diabetes and cancer. No cancer in my immediate family but my brother was recently diagnosed with diabetes. I'm much more like my Dad physiologically, and we have similar issues, with consistent depression being a common thread. He is not an alcoholic but both his parents were. His Mother and at least one of her sister's died of Alzheimer's. My father was diagnosed about a year ago. This is a scary thought for me, and I'm hoping to address the underlying issues sooner than later. I grew up on a pretty standard american diet, lots of fast food and carbs. I feel that my high carb intake was a big part of my initial crash, especially in relation to the digestive issues.

Please let me know if there is any other info that would be helpful.

As Caroline mentioned, early morning sunshine is extremely important. That sunshine is red-shifted, so it is very healing, and looking at the rising sun is a signal for cortisol to spike for the day. Dr K tells us that if our circadian rhythm is off, everything else will be off as well.

I am somewhat addicted to podcasts, and have been able to utilize the iPod in all my jobs for the past several years. I have it in my pocket while I cook, exercise, when I go to sleep. Is this a major concern?
>
> Thanks!

# Thread: Athanay's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/athanays-journal.11887/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 39
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Athanay — 2014-10-13T21:11:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/athanays-journal.11887/post-143731>

Hello kind folks. I have been following Jack's work more recently and am brand new to the forum, this being my first post. I'm fully aware that posting my own problems and asking for suggestions may not be the best introduction to a community such as this. My hope in doing so, aside from getting advice and encouragement from a great forum, is to offer another thread of experience for others to possibly learn from. I have every intention of keeping progress reports along the way, as I continue along the healing path. This place is truly amazing and I hope to be able to contribute more and more as I continue to absorb all of this information. I apologize in advance for the length of this post, and will post the test results and current doctor recommended supplementation at the bottom, should you want to skip the trial and error portion of this essay.
My symptoms started with general digestion issues a few years ago. I had been a vegetarian for several years, and a lazy/uneducated one at times. I ate a lot of carbs in the form of all the bad things we know about these days. I also had stiff muscles and general aches and pains in day to day movement. A pretty intense chronic fatigue began to set in and lingers still, along with the aches and pains. My sleep was erratic at the time and I supplemented with a fair amount of coffee. I just kept crashing over and over again.

An early and limited blood panel (Page 3-5 on attached file '12' - LabCorp/Lauren Johnston), from an allopathic doctor, revealed only a Vit D deficiency, according to her. I have supplemented since, and levels were improved on more recent testing.

I eventually went to a naturopath (over 2 years ago at this point) who focused on chiropractic, nutrition and general detox. She used kinesiology (which never had me all that confident) to tell me I had all sorts of food allergies and leaky gut. She also ran a saliva hormone test (Page 3 on attached file '9' - Diagnos-Techs, Inc) and said I had high estrogen and adrenal exhaustion. This was all new to me so I just had to trust her for the most part. She gave me several supplements (fish oil, adrenal support, zypan and others) and by cutting out many trigger foods my digestion did improve, at least the major discomfort issues. After several months of expensive adjustments and continued fatigue I stopped going. She kept saying adrenal fatigue can take up to 18 months to recover from, but I just loss faith in her for one reason or another. All along I was not eating animal products (ethical reasons + 'supposed' dairy allergy) and was eating heavy grains and legumes, like, all the time.

I moved on to a chinese medicine and acupuncture clinic, but after a few months there was a petering out as well. When I consistently inquired about diet he always just said to eat 'good, nourishing' foods. Just wasn't getting results, aside from the relaxation that often comes from acupuncture.

I eventually started getting more into BulletProof and paleo related dieting and began to consume animal products about a year and half ago. I was hoping for some wow factor, where my shirt would burst open as I sprinted down the street with a grass-fed torch in my hand, no such luck. The transition didn't cause any bad reactions but they didn't seem to help either. After many trials with elimination diets (eggs, dairy, grains etc.) I still can't tell whether I actually have any real allergies/sensitivities. I never get obvious reactions that would indicate a specific allergy, but I have found that certain foods (eggs, salmon, some dairy) consistently make me feel crappy - I get tired and my body feels almost poisoned and tense. I never had food intolerances prior to all this. I eat completely BP/paleo, have been off all wheat/gluten/processed food/corn/soy etc for two years, but still I feel pretty crummy most of the time. Low energy, stiff, scaly skin, dry hair, dark circles under eyes, all the classics. The circles under my eyes in the morning are profound, and my once thick and flowing hair has been reduced to a dry, thin, and frizzy mess.

I started seeing a new naturopath in August of 2013. He did some blood work (Page 1-2 on attached file '12' - LabCorp) and noted that my iron levels were high. He thought this could be causing several of the problems and may be indicative of hemochromatosis. I tried to donate blood (only real way to rid the male body of iron) but had to wait a bit, based on some traveling I did in malaria zones earlier that year. He himself has hemochromatosis and said several of his symptoms were relieved when he began consistently donating blood. He gave me a couple types of digestive enzymes and had put me on pregnenolone to stimulate some basic hormone production, with little results after a few months of use. I have since donated blood a few times, but it hasn't resulted in any major shift in symptoms. I have felt a little better since lowering the iron (more recent tests have shown normal ranges) but again, no major recovery. Not sure what made it so high, but may have been reintroducing large amounts of meat after not eating any for 4-5 years.

I suspect my body might not be digesting fats particularly well, or that my gut is just depleted in general. I went from eating little fat for several years to eating a ton of it over the last year or so. I rarely, if ever, have any digestive discomfort (gas, bloating etc) but until recently I wasn't at all regular with bowel movements, and have had many periods where I only go every 2-3 days.

I used to get better sleep, but still never felt all that rested, but recently my sleep has gotten pretty bad. I can't fall asleep despite being tired and often wake early and can't fall back asleep.

There have been periods where I've had consistent muscle twitching, mostly in legs but other parts of the body as well. It mostly occurs when sitting and especially when laying. Circulation has been poor all along, with legs and arms falling asleep much easier than they used to. Back discomfort is an issue as well.

I have been seeing a new functional medicine doctor in Asheville for the last couple months and he ran a few tests after my evaluation (Page 1-2 on attached file '9' and Page 1-8 on attached file '1') Several B vitamins were quite low along with my DHEA. I was surprised that my cortisol pattern was in normal range, as I seem to go up and down so much with energy throughout the day, and never feel well rested in the morning. He has talked about running a heavy metals screening as well as a comprehensive stool analysis as well.

He has me on the following protocol daily:

\- B-Active
\- Acetyl L-Carnatine
\- DHEA (started with 25mg, now at 75mg)
\- Phenylalanine (started with 500mg, now at 1500mg)
\- A-Lipoic Acid (200-300mg)
\- American Ginseng, Elecampane
\- Probiotic, Digestive Enzymes
\- Magnesium Glycinate
\- Multi

I take/have taken on my own:

\- Krill Oil
\- L-Glutamine
\- Cod Liver Oil
\- Milk Thistle
\- Adaptogenic Herbs
\- Glutathione
\- Probiotics (High Quality)
\- Zinc
\- Manganese

Currently - I have had much more consistency with bowel movements for the last month or so, but still feel that my gut is sluggish. Large meals with protein always seem to bog me down, and eating in general rarely makes me feel better. I have made many a bone broth with mixed results, sometimes it feels soothing but often it makes me feel 'off'. I have tried incorporating much more seafood and feel that it has helped some with overall energy, especially after almost never eating any for many years. Oysters, mussels and a few types of fish have been a welcome addition (with the exception of salmon, which my body seems to reject, having tried multiple times).

I was relieved to hear Jack say that food and supplements aren't enough. I have eaten so well and tried so many products for over 3 years, and still I feel pretty awful most of the time. I'm still dialing in the diet to see what seems to help or hurt, and I feel like fats and proteins are a struggle for me. I was very low carb for awhile but that seemed to strain my body even more, so I have been eating a little (sweet potato mostly) with each meal.

Overall, the consistent feeling I have is that I have been poisoned in some way. No nausea, but my body feels tense and weak. My knees feel stiff and start to ache when I stand for an extended period of time. At times my energy is so low that just moving around feels taxing. I do yoga, bike as much as I can, but most strenuous exercise seems to make me feel worse. I'm trying to get more sun and have been grounding as much as possible, with some noticeable results. I wonder more and more about emf/dirty electricity. I live in a nice home, but I'm on the second floor and have many electronics in my room. I work on a computer most of the day and am starting to think it's stressing my system. I often feel a little better when get out of the house.

I am not all that famaliar with CT and am just now looking more into emf etc. I would very much appreciate anyone who can look at my labs and tell me if anything stands out, beyond what my doctors have noted. The Genova and Solstas tests were the most recent. Everyone has told me that thyroid and other common markers seem normal. The DHEA is definitely low, but so far the supplements recommended have only helped slightly.

I really appreciate all of your time, assuming you made it this far, and hope that some other folks may be able to see this and contribute to the cause. I'm happy to report on my progress and very much look froward to any and all feedback. Just let me know what I can do to help

Many thanks!

### Athanay — 2014-10-13T21:14:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/athanays-journal.11887/post-143732>

My family history has me on a pretty broad spectrum. My Mom's side is more type-A, and has had heart disease, diabetes and cancer. No cancer in my immediate family but my brother was recently diagnosed with diabetes. I'm much more like my Dad physiologically, and we have similar issues, with consistent depression being a common thread. He is not an alcoholic but both his parents were. His Mother and at least one of her sister's died of Alzheimer's. My father was diagnosed about a year ago. This is a scary thought for me, and I'm hoping to address the underlying issues sooner than later. I grew up on a pretty standard american diet, lots of fast food and carbs. I feel that my high carb intake was a big part of my initial crash, especially in relation to the digestive issues.

### Athanay — 2014-10-27T16:11:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/athanays-journal.11887/post-145302>

Just a quick update.

I have been grounding as much as possible in the morning and throughout the day. It always feels nice to touch the ground, and I feel it may be helping counter the all day computer cycle. I'm also eating quality fish daily, including oysters. I have felt a little 'lighter' in general, which is always a relief. Getting much more sun as well, which has been something I've lacked for a long time - again, always feels good to feel the sun on your skin.

My sleep is still not great, but has improved a little over the past couple weeks. I'm moving into a one story house this week, and will be curious to see if it makes a difference. I realized recently that every house I've lived in since moving to my current location has either had a basement, or I was on the second floor (sometimes with a basement, so technically third floor) Really hoping my new place will have me better grounded.

Any thoughts on sleeping outside for a few days? It's getting cold here, so may be a good way to get mild CT as well?

I'm looking to move more into CT, and also wondering about the leptin reset. Is this something everyone should do?

Where will I find the specific guidelines for CT and leptin rx etc?

Hope everyone is as good as they can be

### Athanay — 2015-02-09T19:13:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/athanays-journal.11887/post-155143>

Long overdue update. Hope everyone is well!

I did the 23andme testing, and would highly recommend that others do the same. I discovered a few things that were definitely hindering the healing process. Apparently I burn through B12 quickly and so need a steady supply to keep the energy flowing. I was a vegetarian for many years, and was not supplementing B12 at all, so I imagine that did a number on my system. Also, I don't metabolize sulphur all that efficiently, so all those larger meals with heavy veggies were actually taxing my system a bit - which makes sense considering I almost always felt subdued after eating full meals. I have since spread out the servings, and along with Methyl B12 and Methyl Folate, I have seen an increase in overall energy. I was also encouraged to take SAMe for some serotonin issues related to MTHFR. All in all, it seems to be helping. The doctor I'm seeing is Dan Stickler, who has had Jack on his podcast (formerly 'Living the Optimized Life', now called 'Biohacking for Optimal Health' - lots of good information) so I feel good about his general approach to health. I had also been taking magnesium glycinate, but it turns out glycinate is not good for my system and have switched to malate. It's amazing how long it can take to fully comprehend N=1. I would try all these recs from different practitioners and despite feeling sometimes worse, would continue to try them, based on all the 'evidence' of their efficacy. I'm slowly but surely learning to really listen to my body, day to day, and to just be more patient with all this experimentation.

I'm currently working on stress reduction, using neurofeedback, and an Emwave 2 that I purchased. Lots of seafood down the hatch, but still feel that I have some gut issues, as I still rarely feel 'better' after eating. I'm doing low fodmap for the first time, along with some more elimination in the diet with suspect foods including eggs, all dairy, and unfortunately coffee. I really love coffee, but have found that almost every time I consume it, I eventually (within an hour or so) feel pretty gross. I get stiff and achey, and my energy tends to drop. I'm not sure what I'm reacting to there, or if it's just not in the cards for me for now.

My gut concerns have almost always been around constipation. I don't feel constipated, I'm just not going to the bathroom consistently - every couple days, sometimes longer, despite eating 2 square meals a day. I can tell there is just a lack of flow in my system, in general. I've been making bone broths and am about to start some more probiotics. I am also dabbling a bit with resistant starch, no real adverse effects so far. Maybe a little gas, but my dreams have been profoundly epic - the story lines seem to go on for ages, like one long dream, rather than several broken up pieces. I have heard of this occurrence, but am curious as to the mechanism, and whether it is something to view as positive/negative/who knows?

I feel that general dehydration may be a major issue for me, some of which is probably coming from consistent emf exposure (many hours a day on the computer, room close to refrigerator, roommate mostly unwilling to turn of wifi at night - the list goes on). My skin is profoundly dry, including scalp/hair. I drink RO water throughout the day, but am reading more into structured water, after hearing about dehydration occurring despite the amount of water you consume. I know it's a big part of the equation with Jack's work, but am struggling to make it work. One thing worth noting is that I rarely feel thirsty, and think I mostly drink water out of habit/protocol.

I have a had a lot of muscle twitching over the past year or so, but have seen a decline over the past couple months. My feeling is that more grounding and less technology has helped. I have a tri-meter and a stetzerizer meter, which has helped me steer clear of problem areas. My outlets show really high with the Stetzerizer, but I felt that using the filters was actually making it worse. The number dropped, but when using several filters in my bedroom, my body seemed to be pulsing a bit more, especially when trying to sleep. I have read that faulty wiring issues can be exacerbated by the filters, so I've been going without for the time being.

The past week or two has shown some improvement in sleep. For me it's falling asleep that is the issue, but lately I have increased exercise and have a nighttime routine that seems to be effective. I do think my room is a major source of emf. When I lay down at night, my eyes often water a bit, and they have clearly continued to do so throughout the night, as I often wake with dried tears around my eyes. I still never feel well rested, but am hoping consistent exercise will continue to assist in that arena.

I began CT about 3 weeks ago. I ordered replacement ice sheets for the ice vest and place them in between two compression shirts at night for about 45 minutes. It's not a challenge in the slightest, and I'm wondering when I should switch to the baths. I feel good about CT and have noticed that I'm the only person in my town cruis_ing_ through the icy cold mountains with my windows down, and I'm comfortable. Only other difference is that I can tell my car is a taxing source of emf. My leg muscles twitch after being in the car for a short period of time.

The best news I have involves my plans in the coming months. I am moving to Colorado for the spring/summer and will be staying in a small mountain town. I will be alone, and will have complete control of the variables in the immediate environment. I can shut off wifi at will, kill the breaker at night, sleep under the stars, climb mountains, live by candle light etc. etc. I think it will be the best thing I can do for my myself, and am really curious to see how this environment will affect my ability to heal. I've already worked it out with my local seafood/meat distributor to ship me whatever I may need. I'll still be working on a computer, but the overall environment should be much cleaner than what I'm dealing with now. Antenna search.com shows my current location having 26 Towers and 254 antennas within a 4 mile radius. The new location shows 3 towers and 4 antennas! I'm not expecting a miracle, but I always feel better when I spend even a short amount of time there.

In the mean time, I have the option to move into a new place for the next month or so, until I leave. I'd be on the second floor. but am considering the move, as I'm fairly convinced my current house is a stressor, emf, possible mold etc.

I welcome any comments or suggestions, especially in relation to increased CT, constipation, and housing. Thanks for all the support, hope you are all _improving in your own ways!_

Questions:

1\. Dehydration - how to experiment, drink more/less/different types of water?
2\. Anyone else had luck with resistant starch for improving digestion?
3\. Been using ice sheets for about 3 weeks, am I safe to start CT baths?
4\. Second-story living - or should I stay put for the next month or so?

### Martin — 2015-02-13T15:20:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/athanays-journal.11887/post-155528>

9-14 milligauss is horrendous. But it will drop some as you back away.

Try holding the meter at the same distance as your body parts, like hands when you're on the keyboard, or your face when you are in a normal viewing position. That will give you the gauss level that you are chronically exposed to, as opposed to having your face right up on the screen. What are your readings when your body is in a normal position?

ex-screen licker,
NSH

### Martin — 2015-02-14T18:25:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/athanays-journal.11887/post-155612>

Let us know what setting your meter is on. Also, where are your power lines and circuit breaker panel? Is your place a house or apartment? Age of structure? The older they are, more of a chance someone's goofed the wiring up. Test your breaker panel from about a foot away, and more, to see how the readings fall off. Another experiment is to turn off the main breaker and THEN go through your place testing the fields. That could show you if it's coming from power lines or some other external source other than the house .

Tough call on the 4G and other stuff. Hard to tell without measuring with _accurate_ RF meter and gauss meter.

ex-panel box licker,
NSH

### Martin — 2015-02-19T20:52:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/athanays-journal.11887/post-156076>

What does your bed read? You can position it to get some distance from the stronger fields if turning off the breakers isn't enough. In your potential new digs, check the floor of the 2nd story for fields. Often there are things like fluorescent lights on the ceiling of the 1st floor that can greatly affect the upper floor. And basements can house hot water heaters and lights that can have strong fields. Mag fields can go right through walls that are not steel, and even then can penetrate. Distance and metals, like nickel, are the two best options for reducing. Nickel sheet metal is off-the-damn-chart expensive! Thick sheets of steel (3/8"-1/2") can be somewhat effective, but hard to work with.

NSH

### ketochi — 2014-10-14T04:25:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketochis-journal.11889/post-143765>

Hi All,

Where to begin? 35 year old male that has been dealing with tremor and neuropathy along with mood/sleep issues for roughly 15 years. Conventional neurology is now telling me I might have MS although I currently show no lesions. The unofficial MS diagonosis led me to Dr Terry Wahls and her Wahls protocol which I started the ketogenic verison of 2 weeks ago. I've also been to see a functional Dr who ran several tests of which the following appears noteworthy:

-   Heterozygous for MTHFR C677T and A1298C - since started supplementing with methylfolate and methyl b12
-   high cortisol levels at night - I was doing shift work at the time of this test and my sleep cycle was a bit messed up. I'm back on a normal schedule so I plan to retest this soon to see if anything has changed
-   High levels mercury/copper - I took the heavy metals test fully expecting the results to come back normal. No clue how I would have aquired high levels of these metals although I've since decided on an oral chelation protocol and with the new diet/methylation support hopefully these levels come down.
-   Food allergies - seems to be the usual culprits - eggs, soy, dairy, etc...eliminated these from my diet
-   High testosterone levels - like really high. 1450 on a 200-1200 scale. No clue what to do about this or even if should do anything. The endo that ran the tests really has no explanation.

I've been in research mode focusing on the ketogenic diet mainly which in turn led me to Dr Kruse. I recently watched an interview of his with Primal Edge Health and have since read the book and been all over the website. I can say for certain at this point that Dr Kruse is a total nut and I mean that as the highest compliment . I like people that think outside the box and realize I need to start thinking this way if I am ever to get my health back. To be honest, Dr K's food pyramid scares me quite a bit. Since I supposedly have high levels of mercury I've been avoiding seafood (not hard as I've never been a seafood eater). I've decided to add some sockeye salmon to my diet and see how it goes. Since I've started my detox I been doing 4 sauna sesssions a week. How well it helps with the detox is up for debate but it's relaxing for me so I'll keep at it. What I've always had issues with is the cold and so I'm excited about CT. Hoping that becoming cold adapted will help with the adrenals and sleep. Time will tell.

I look forward to reading and contributing as I go.

# Thread: Stridar's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stridars-journal.11897/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Stridar — 2014-10-15T02:24:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stridars-journal.11897/post-143857>

Hi all!

My name is Stridar and I am a 25 year old female living down in Sydney. I've been following Jack's work for a while and it's only just begun to sink in, and I'm really excited to be a part of this community! While I’m not obese, I do have a pot belly and my goal is to slim it right down. I’m 163 cm and 59 kilos (130 lb) and I want to drop approximately 5 kilos (11 lb). I also have PCOS, which I also want to treat and get my labs optimal in general.

I fall asleep within five minutes of going to bed, sleep peacefully without waking up and I don’t feel fatigued waking up or during the day. However I suspect my life-long pattern of getting 5 hours of sleep contributed to my muffin top; that and my prior addiction to instant noodles, rice and overall poor diet.

I have a computer desk job which exposes me to EMF during the daytime, which means I’ll need to be stricter with my environment control. Luckily, I live in the outer suburbs of Sydney and not directly within the city where I work.

I’ll be following the leptin prescription, epi-paleo diet and getting my circadian cycle normalised to the best of my ability to lose my gut and counter my PCOS. Here are the main changes I’ve been implementing:

\- I’ve been using my Lumie clock for about three months. I plan on weaning off using my Lumie eventually, because I don’t like the potential risk of EMF. But we’ll see.

\- I’ve skipped breakfast for the majority of my life. Now, I’ve started off the day drinking grass-fed protein shake to start the day. I am working on getting 6 oysters and eggs for breakfast, but meanwhile I’ll have at least a protein shake for breakfast.

\- I’m packing vegetable soup with canned sardines for work every day. Sometimes I order sashimi instead of packing soup and sardines.

\- After sunset, I wear my blue blocker glasses and have my Himalayan salt lamp as the only source of light, to keep my room as dark as possible.

\- Been doing CT with my Cool Fat Burner for an hour almost every night. I try to take bitter melon extract before the CT session but sometimes I forget. Been drinking glasses of cold water to amp the CT. All my showers are in cold water too.

\- I’ve ceased using my computer from 9:00pm onwards. Instead of wasting time on Facebook, I’m now drawing or reading until bedtime which is 10:30-11:00pm, with a 6:30am start.

\- I’ve been eating two dozen oysters over the weekend. Sometimes four dozen when I can afford it (is this an excessive amount to consume over 2-3 days?)

\- I’ve switched from regular fluoride water to RO bottled water, however the RO water is sourced from Australian waters which is not ideal. I am currently searching for an affordable brand/supplier of bottled spring water from the northern hemisphere, any Aussies here know of any brands?

\- I’ve been trying to get as much sun as possible. I sit outside in the sun for lunches at work with my barefeet on the grass and for a bit longer on the weekends. However I am concerned about the donut hole UV, so I’m not sure how much sun I really should be getting or limiting. I’ve begun taking 5000 UI vitamin D from Life Extension everyday for extra precaution. Easier than gulping five 1000 UI tablets, that’s for sure.

\- I’m in the progress of reducing EMF in my household too, one step at a time.

\- I know you’re not supposed to exercise while on the leptin reset, but I just began doing HIIT on my treadmill for 20 minutes, once a week in the late afternoon. Surely this cannot hurt?

\- I’m using a wet sauna for 15 minutes twice a week. Just started last week and I love it.

\- I don’t feel stressed at the moment. Sometimes I feel stressed to meet a work deadline but I don’t believe it’s anything too strenuous. I typically use my EmWave daily. I also meditate on weekends, though I’ll be working to factor meditation every morning fairly soon.

Lab results:

DHEAS: 5.2 umol/L (I have no idea how to convert this number to compare it to Jack’s Optimizing Labs PDF)

Total Protein: 77g/L

Vitamin D: 104 nmol/L

Glucose: 4.3 mmol/L

Globuline: 32 g/L

Creatinine: 65 umol/L

Neutrophils: 1.73

Highly Sensitive CRP: <0.4 mg/L

Triglycerides: 0.7 mmol/L

HDL Chol: 1.8 mmol/L

LDL Chol: 5.2 mmol/L

Cholesterol: 7.3 mmol/L

My cholesterol is my biggest concern because it’s risen quite a bit, it was only 4.9 last year prior to going paleo/epi-paleo. My doctor believes the rise to be due to the grass-fed butter and coconut oil I’ve been taking over the year, but honestly I do not believe I consume an excessive amount. I put a little butter in my green tea in the morning, eat duck fat on the weekends and use coconut oil in my cooking but all in moderate amounts. What should I do to lower my cholesterol, and should I even be worried?

I need more labs, I know. My doctor does not believe I require detailed blood results because I’m not seriously ill, so I’m looking for a place in Sydney that offers comprehensive lab results for an affordable price; if anyone recommends a place let me know.

P.S If you read through that essay and made it through, I applaud you!

I don't have a very good view from my house. I've been waking up at 6ish pretty regularly now, sometimes I just lie in bed peacefully before the alarm goes off. Instead of lying in bed, I may start forcing myself to go outside and sit on my front lawn grass barefooted to gaze at the sunrise/sky/whatever-I-can-get.

It's kinda weird, I don't konk out as quickly as I did before, which was as soon as my head hit my pillow. Now it takes 10-15 minutes to fall asleep. So I think I've been sleep deprived my entire life but didn't feel the symptoms and therefore didn't pay it any attention. I used to sleep through the night without waking but now I typically wake up at 4am in a half sleepy/dazed state for half a minute and return to sleep. Not sure what's going on there but I'm not too worried.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-20T03:19:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stridars-journal.11897/post-144359>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=143929)
>
> Welcome from me too, nice to have you here
>
> I have high cholesterol too but I am not one bit worried

When calcium levels are altered in the CSF and the blood, cholesterol in the basal skin cannot be sulfated from cysteine stores. Sulfur stores come from things I mentioned in the EPCOT Rx, seafood and pork. Cholesterol sulfate is manufactured by a combination of specific frequencies of

(1) ultraviolet radiation

(2) visible light radiation

(3) infrared radiation

(4) and a range of oxygen saturation

The sulfate actually comes from homocysteine in RBC’s within hemoglobin. This is why elevated homocysteine and low vitamin D levels are a non specific risk factors for heart disease and stroke. Homocysteine is the source of sulfate. You have to consume “some energy” to produce a sulfate from homocysteine, and the red blood cells actually supply the ATP to the plaque. ATP withdraws electrons from homocysteine to sulfate the cholesterol in blood. Many people forget that RBC have no nuclei or mitochondria so you might be wondering where the ATP comes from? Because of the lack of nuclei and mitochondria, mature red blood cells are incapable of generating energy via the Krebs cycle. Instead, erythrocytes depend on the anaerobic conversion of glucose by the Embden-Meyerhof pathway for the generation and storage of high-energy phosphates. Moreover, erythrocytes possess a unique glycolytic bypass for the production of 2,3-bisphosphoglycerate (2,3-DPG), the Rapoport-Luebering shunt. This shunt bypasses the phosphoglycerate kinase (PGK) step and accounts for the synthesis and regulation of 2,3-DPG levels that decrease hemoglobin's affinity for oxygen. This works in concert with the magnetic flux forces that allow paramagnetic diatomic oxygen to be drawn to mitochondria. In this way, 2,3-DPG constitutes a quantum energy buffer to catalyze this reaction. This frees sulfate to bind with cholesterol in blood plasma. When your LDL cholesterol is elevated it is a sign your skin is lacking sunlight in these frequencies. These frequencies occur 10-12 noon in most places on the globe.

When you sulfate cholesterol, you turn it into both a water-soluble and a fat-soluble molecule. This allows it to get into places using water and fat pathways in cells. It doesn't have to be packaged up inside a low-density lipoprotein (LDL) particle to be used by cells. When sunlight interacts with eNOS and cholesterol some quantum magic happens in skin. So what is the key to high LDL cholesterol? CALCIUM EFFLUX.

### Josh — 2014-10-18T03:37:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-6.11899/post-144147>

> **Abstract**
> The formation of amyloid fibrils proceeds via a nucleation-dependent mechanism in which nucleation phase is generally associated with a high free energy resulting in the rate-limiting step. On the basis of this kinetic feature, the nucleation is one of the most crucial phases controlling the pathogenesis of amyloidoses, but little is known about the details of how protein molecules and surrounding environment vary at this stage. Here, we applied near infrared (NIR) spectral monitoring of water structural changes in real time during the nucleation-dependent fibrillation of insulin. Whilst multivariate spectral analysis in the 2050–2350 nm spectral region indicated cross-β formation, characteristic transformations of water structure have been detected in the spectral region 1300–1600 nm corresponding to the first overtone of water OH stretching vibrations. Furthermore, specific water spectral patterns (aquagrams) related to different water molecular conformations have been found along the course of protein nucleation and aggregation. Right in the beginning, dissociation of hydrogen-bonded network in bulk water and coinstantaneous protein and ion hydration were observed, followed by water hydrogen-bonded networks development, presumably forcing the nucleation. These specific transformations of water spectral pattern could be used further as a biomarker for early non-invasive diagnosis of amyloidoses prior to explosive amplification and deposits of amyloid fibrils.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**Schematic illustration representing multi-step transformation of water structures during the fibril formation.**

> **Figure S6**
> **The structural transformation of water molecules as estimated by PLS concentration regression analysis.** The analysis was performed with a dataset of NIR spectra in the region of 1300–1600 nm monitored at four different concentrations of insulin protein; 0.5, 1, 3, and 5 mg/ml. (A) Regression vector obtained as a model for the prediction of insulin concentration. The regression vector was based on the spectra at 6–10 min. The most prominent absorbance band was exhibited at around 1455 nm (assigned to S2), and additionally, positive peaks at 1365 nm (water solvation shell) and 1381 nm (OH−·(H2O)5 and/or O2−·(H2O)4), and negative peaks at 1421 nm (hydration), and 1437 nm (H+·(H2O)2) were observed, suggesting that hydrogen-bonded water structures are involved strongly with the formation of amyloid fibrils. (B) Time-dependent change in the SEP of protein concentration. In this analysis, the spectra at 6–10 min and 1–60 min were used for the model and test datasets, respectively. Although a markedly larger value at 1–5 min might indicate spectral change associated with temperature jump, the gradual increase in SEP values in the range of 11 min to 30 min coincides roughly with the elongation phase, verifying the change in amount of S2 water species. (C) Time-dependent change of the ratio of absorbance at 1460 nm (assigned to hydrogen-bonded water, S2) and that at 1410 nm (assigned to free water, S0). The result of protein solution is plotted by closed circles and that of solvent (25 mM HCl containing 100 mM NaCl) is also shown by open circles for reference. Immediately after starting the monitoring, because of the temperature increase and adjustment linked to increase of less hydrogen bonded water (S0), the overall ratio decreased. After 5 min, the value of absorbance ratio seemed to increased, which agreed very well with the observed 2 stages in the nucleation stage ([Figure 4](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4094474/figure/pone-0101997-g004/)). Further on, in the next 2–3 minutes, it decreased very slightly and increased again showing details of the elongation phase (phase II). With this, we show that the changes in the initial stage of the fibril formation reaction are in accordance with the PCA and PLS regression results for protein concentration measurement.

(TIF)

> If it is assumed that the organized hydrogen-bonded water network is not restricted within the proximal region but extended to considerably distal ones, it is likely that the water network contributes to interplay among an ensemble of protein molecules regulating well-ordered assembling. In light of a novel concept which has been recently proposed on the basis of explicit-water molecular dynamics simulations[\[13\]](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4094474/#pone.0101997-Chong1), water-mediated attraction between protein molecules contributes to directional adjustment towards a stable associated structure, which will lead to nucleation in the present case. After the nucleation, the hydrogen-bonded water conformations seemed to be attenuated along with abrupt amplification of fibrils during the elongation phase, and again developed after reaching the equilibrium phase.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4094474/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4094474/)

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-18T15:24:30+0300

I have mentioned many times on my blog and forums and on our FB groups that the mainstream ancestral people don't get the " atomic scale argument" when it comes to things like iodine and T3 and T4. One of the great things about seafood is that it contains selenium which helps convert thyroid hormones to active T3. While you can eat more carbs to increase free T3 levels (a common paleo recommendation), this increases the need for iodine even more. This creates a huge issue chronically especially in the CSF. ([http://www.medical-hypotheses....](http://www.medical-hypotheses.com/.../S0306-9877.../abstract\)). Most of the T4 is stored in our gut, so by eating seafood, you allow form to meet function as the seafood can permeate the gut where majority of T4 is stored, allowing for the conversion of more T4 to T3." While DHA has pretty amazing quantum effects, as Bill has talked about here before, it needs to be paired with iodine (as that's the HUMAN body's preferred antioxidant system) to protect DHA from oxidation. This occurs in synapses due to this Grotthuss mechanism especially for heat transfers. So, we've come full circle. You can either eat seafood and get a great evolutionary package of things like DHA, iodine and selenium, which will support thyroid health, or you can follow the traditional paleo path lacking in seafood DHA and iodine and continually up your carb intake to offset this loss. The "leadership of ancestral health" needs to tell people this will trigger a need for more iodine from their diet and since you're not getting it in "their" diet, your body will divert to backup antioxidant systems like vitamin C, vitamin E and glutathione. NO one is saying this because it hurts their business models. Now you see why so many young paleo folks follow "their thought leaders" eat lots of carbs and take supplements like glutathione and vitamin C year-round. They're forcing their bodies to rely on a suboptimal pathway which requires this constant band-aid approach.

### nonchalant — 2014-10-21T02:17:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-6.11899/post-144490>

> Symmetry is broken by any phase transition in chemistry. Any time symmetry is broken, **energy and information transfers must occur by nature’s laws.** This occurs many times in biochemical reaction of cells. And as such, all breaks of symmetry require a transfer of energy by the laws of physics to satisfy the Second Law of Thermodynamics. Symmetry is also broken any time temperature rises or falls or when electrons or protons are moving in any biochemical reaction. Any transfer of energy has the potential to break symmetry and therefore to give rise to emergent properties in the protein polymers or products of these reactions.

This part seems very important. Anytime we CT, we cause phase transitions, break symmetry, transfer energy and information. And we see emergent properties in our proteins, like helix formation perhaps.

Not PC, but it gives some perspective on Choice, Chance and such.....

> A Jew went to a whorehouse and told the madam that he wanted the cheapest girl in the place.
> 'We have a black girl for ten dollars,' she told him.
> 'But I only have four dollars,' he protested.
> After a long argument the madam consented to take him on herself for four dollars.
> The Jew did not return to the whorehouse for another ten years. When he did, the madam greeted him cordially and told him that as a result of their relationship years ago, he had a nine-year-old son. She called the boy out and introduced him to his father.
> 'So you are my dad,' said the boy. 'You know, ever since I was born I've wondered what my last name was. Tell me, dad, what is my last name?'
> 'Goldberg,' the father replied.
> 'My god!' exclaimed the boy, 'you mean I am jewish?'
> 'Don't knock it, boy,' he replied. 'If I had had six dollars more ten years ago you would have been black too.'

> A pregnant jewish girl asked her doctor what position she would have to lie in to give birth to her baby.
> 'The same position you were in when you started it,' the doctor told her.
> 'My god!' she exclaimed. 'Do you mean I will have to drive around Berlin in a taxi for two hours with my feet hanging out the window?'

This piezoelectric and protonicity signals are sent everywhere within our bodies at the speed of light to transfer energy and information to your body. ### PaulG — 2014-10-29T04:32:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-6.11899/post-145506>

Anyone interested in how H+ ions work in the mitochondria should read carefully Ling's 1981 68 page paper "[Oxidative Phosphorylation and Mitochondrial Physiology: A Critical Review of Chemiosmotic Theory, and Reinterpretation by the Association-Induction Hypothesis](http://www.physiologicalchemistryandphysics.com/pdf/PCP13-29_ling.pdf)"

Like most of Ling, it is not a quick and easy read but well worth persevering and plenty of nuggets (proton gradients, Mg2\+ and Ca2 vs swelling/shrinkage, mitochondrial electrical potential) in there which I am sure @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) will circle back to down the pike...

> SUBJECT OUTLINE
> I. INTRODUCTION
> 11\. THE CHEMIOSMOTIC HYPOTHESIS
> A. Generation of an electrical potential difference by postulated electrogenic pump vs. law of macroscopic electroneutrality
> B. Proton gradient
> C. Electrical potential gradient
> 1\. In vivo measurements
> a. Membrane disruption
> b. Electrode misplacement in the intermembrane space
> c. Inpocketing of mitochondrial membrane at microelectrode tip
> 2\. Insitu measurements
> 3\. Effect of valinomycin on resistance
> D. Is the mitochondrial inner membrane impermeable?
> 1\. Is there enough phospholipid in the inner membrane to form a continuous bilayer barrier?
> 2\. Is the mitochondrial inner membrane virtually impermeable to ions in general and H+ in particular?
> a. H+ permeability
> b. Cation impermeability
> (I) Inner membrane permeability to K+ and Nu+. (2) Inner membrane permeability to divalent cations.
> c. Anion impermeability
> E. Functions of uncoupling agents and ionophores
> F. ATP synthesis
> G. Conclusions regarding the chemiosmotic hypothesis
> 111\. THEORY OF THE LIVING CELL
> A. Basic features of the membrane-pump theory
> (I) Water. (2) Ions. (3) Cell surface barrier. (4) Membrane pumps. (5) Cell volume.
> (6) Resting potential.
> B. Basic features of the association-induction hypothesis
> (I) Water. (2) Ions. (3) Cell surface barrier. (4) Pumps and solute exclusion.
> (5) Cell volume. (6) Resting potential.
> C. Discriminatory experimental evidence
> 1\. Energy requirements of pumps
> 2\. Membrane vs. cytoplasm as the seat of discrimination in solute distribution
> 3\. The adsorbed state of K+
> 4\. Consequences of K+ binding in living cells
> IV. THE SOURCE OF ENERGY FOR ATP SYNTHESIS
> V. PROTEINS ACCORDING TO THE A1 HYPOTHESIS : HIGH- AND LOW-ENERGY STATES OF PROTEIN-ION-WATER SYSTEMS. PRIMARY INDUCTIVE EFFECT
> A. Inductive effect as the basis of energy and information transfer over distance
> 1\. Universal applicability of the inductive effect
> 2\. Target groups of the inductive effect
> a. The acid dissociation constant (or pK,)
> b. H-bonding strength
> c. Oxidation-reduction potential
> 3\. The inducing "groups"
> 4\. Additivity and reversibility of the inductive effect
> B. c -Value concept, linear model, and theoretically calculated change in preference for K+, Na+, H+, and NH4+
> C. Complex interaction between biologically active agents
> D. Cooperative behavior of living cells: interpretation based on the A1 hypothesis
> 1\. Cooperative adsorption isotherm of Yang and Ling
> 2\. Oxygen dissociation of hemoglobin and related phenomena
> a. Empirical analysis (Hill's equation)
> b. The AAKM theories
> c. The Yang-Ling model: comparison with other models
> 3\. Three types of autocooperative adsorption on proteins
> E. Autocooperativity in selective solute accumulation in living cells
> F. Control by cardinal adsorbents of shifts between two cooperative states
> VI. TENTATIVE MODEL OF ASSOCIATIVE-INDUCTIVE COUPLING MECHANISM FOR ELECTRON TRANSPORT AND OXIDATIVE PHOSPHORYLATION
> A. The coupling mechanism hypothesis
> B. Comparison with other models
> 1\. Heme-heme interaction and the Bohr effect
> 2\. Oxidation-reduction controlled autocooperative ion adsorption shifts
> a. Cytochrome c
> b. Hemoglobin
> c. Pyridine nucleotides
> VII. INTERPRETATION OF OTHER MITOCHONDRIAL PROPERTIES UNDER THE A1 HYPOTHESIS
> A. Additional basic A1 concepts
> 1\. Physical state of the bulk of cell water in living cells
> 2\. Swelling and shrinking
> 3\. Polarized water rather than membrane-lipid as seat of selective permeability
> 4\. Resting potential as surface adsorption potential
> B. Important findings in mitochondrial physiology and some new interpretations
> 1\. Swelling and its reversal by ATP
> a. Type I. Simple swelling and shrinkage
> b. Type 11. Dissociative swelling
> c. Type 111. Depolarizing swelling
> 2\. Reappraisal of previous reports according to the A1 hypothesis
> a. ATP and swelling/shrinkage of fetal and adult rat liver mitochondria
> b. Mg+ and Caw vs. swelling/shrinkage
> c. Passive osmotic swelling
> 3\. Ion and substrate transport
> 4\. Uncouplers, ionophores, Ca+, Mg+, ATP, and other agents as cardinal adsorbents
> a. Mechanism of action of valinomycin on mitochondrial K+ accumulation
> b. Ionophores, uncouplers, and other cardinal adsorbents: induction of c-value change as basis of their action on mitochondrial ion distribution
> (I) Respiration and anaerobiosis. (2) "Ionophores." (3) Uncouplers (4) Thiol reagents. (5) ATP and ADP.
> 5\. Synchronous oscillatory changes in mitochondrial swelling, ion uptake, and other properties
> 6\. Mitochondria1 electrical potential

# Thread: Newbie from the UK!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-the-uk.11903/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5

### healthyuk — 2014-10-15T13:38:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-the-uk.11903/post-143914>

Hi All
Have been reading the forum for quite a while but have only just been able to post because of problems with the forum. I am trying to upload a pic but keep getting an error message, so will contact support.
I have started reading all the blogs - great reading but a lot to take in  Eat seafood twice a day everyday. Try to ground for 1 hr everyday and limit blue light & have switched off the wifi! And walk in the local woods most days.

So i have a few questions please if anyone can help would be very appreciated!

1\. I would like to download the Epcot rx, is this still up to date/current? Or is their a later version?

2\. I eat this trout [http://www.abelandcole.co.uk/rainbow-trout-fillets-200g](http://www.abelandcole.co.uk/rainbow-trout-fillets-200g)
and this salmon [http://www.tesco.com/groceries/product/details/?id=279468791](http://www.tesco.com/groceries/product/details/?id=279468791)
Can i eat these raw?

3\. This is the water i drink all the time [http://www.tesco.com/groceries/product/details/?id=259614228](http://www.tesco.com/groceries/product/details/?id=259614228)
Is this brand okay or are their better options.

4\. Can someone please point me in the right direction of the best place for someone in the UK to purchase a magnetico? Benefits or any particular type to buy?

5\. I am thinking of removing fat from my diet and eating solely fish. Is this a good idea or do i need to keep with the fat please? Can you live only on seafood?

6\. I have an electric meter in the bedroom and am currently in the process of getting it moved outside. I assume this will bring great benefits when done

7\. Would feeding my pets (cats & dogs) a seafood heavy diet give them the same benefits as us (humans)

8\. What is the one most important part of Jacks protocols? Grounding? CT? Removal of blue light? or something else?

Sorry for all the questions & hopefully some of you wont mind answering them

Thankyou!
HealthyUK

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-15T14:44:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-the-uk.11903/post-143920>

1\. Epcot Rx is fine.
2\. You could but you don't have to.
3\. Its fine if you get it in glass. If not stick to spring water or RO water in glass.
4\. No idea. I say craig-list/ebay of contact magnetico directly.
5\. Extremely bad idea.
6\. That is really bad.........it should never be in your house of directly outside of it.
7\. Yes. Both are carnivores.
8\. The most important thing is limiting blue light, avoiding non native EMF and adding back enough DHA to offset your losses from the non native EMF and blue light

### Inger — 2014-10-15T16:34:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-the-uk.11903/post-143926>

Welcome, HealthyUK!

love Jack's answers

My cat eats mostly shrimp, salmon, herring... and some land meat.. that is. She is doing so great! Even if 15 yo.. her fur is so shiny and she is a happy girl

### louise — 2014-10-15T16:53:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-the-uk.11903/post-143928>

hi healthyuk, love the questions and loved the answers even more........my 11yr old retriever loves his raw diet and is way less stiff in his back legs since I changed him over a few months back.
i live in wales and also walk in woods everyday....what a great way to get healthy huh!!!!!

### caroline — 2014-10-16T00:05:57+0300

# Thread: B vitamins and lethargy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/b-vitamins-and-lethargy.11905/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-17T18:57:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-mitochondria-electromagnetic-organs.11921/post-144114>

DHA turns light into an electrical signal. When DHA is missing the electrical signal is missing in CM and tissues. What are the implication of this? Mitochondria have two CM and a matrix filled with H+. This is where mass charges are saved. Without DHA you can no longer use them as a super capacitor of electric charges. Why is this big? Without a charge is alters the masses of the subatomic particles within mitochondria. This is physics 101. This also points out why modern obesity researchers fundamentally clueless about fat. None of them are aware that according to Einstein's mass equivalence equation (E=mc2) that the mass of subatomic particles is an energenic variable that is subject to the electric charge of the surrounding environment; that is why the universal constant for gravitation has been shown to consistently inconstant by physics experiments when they have decided to measured it in space. Well that science scales to the surrounding environment in our mitochondria too. These laws are universal. No one seems to realize it. This implies that as our mitochondria lose their electric charge because DHA and iodine are missing in action, masses of all subatomic particles in mitochondria increases as well. At a macroscopic level where biology scale resides, we observe increases in our weight gain because we are losing energy from the interior of the mitochondrial matrix as ECT slows. As ECT slows electric charge drops. Hyperlipid has been pounding this point in his proton series for months. No one gets the implications. This is why we humans get fat fundamentally.

The less ideal your mitochondria hold their electric charges, the higher the fat version of the keto diet has to be. This is why Jimmy Moore can't handle carbs but most of the rest of us can. I would remind you all how does Jimmy make his $$$? TECHNOLOGY. He can never access autophagy to recycle his shot mitochondria. Guys like Ben Greenfield has bad mitochondria because of excessive endurance exercise but he can lower his fat content and use carbs because he can recycle mitochondria with a lack of electric charge. As your mitonchondria improve, the lower the fat content needs to be; as the protein levels can increase........as you improve further the protein levels in your diet should decrease because your ubiquination pathways improve so you don't not need to replace a lot of protein in your tissues. Bill has pointed out that people who are big time exerciser's don't need a lot of protein. This is 180 degree opposite the paleo idea. They love bacon and crossfitting. As you improve further, you begin to be able to exercise more, building your muscle mass as well and then you can add "seasonal carbs" back to your template with out risk. This is the part the low carb people do not understand. The more you exercise the less protein you actually need. This is another thing that the paleo kids do not understand because their "leaders" have no idea how ubiquination rates are tied to the amount of exercise you do. Ask Bill Lagakos if this is bad advice. I do not want anyone buying what I am selling. I want you to all know why I say what I do. My understanding is based upon a different "scale of science" than paleo biochemistry.

Moreover, this explains the mTOR controversy in aging too.

### BTA — 2014-10-17T22:25:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-mitochondria-electromagnetic-organs.11921/post-144123>

consuming a high fat diet overactives the mTOR pathway? Leading to a wide range of health problems and less muscle growth?

A study by Rivas et al.7 confirmed that consuming a high-fat diet triggers insulin insensitivity, yielding diminished mTOR signaling and muscle protein synthesis. mTOR inhibition stems from negative feedback within the insulin signaling pathway. Chronic consumption of a high-fat diet overactivates mTOR, which in turn heavily activates the molecule S6K. A hyperactive S6K produces inactivating signals within the insulin-signaling cascade, thus diminishing sensitivity to insulin along with mTOR activation and protein synthesis.

1\. Hawley JA, Gibala MJ, et al. J Sports Sci 2007; 25 Suppl 1, S115-124.2. Walker DK, Dickinson JIM, et al. Med Sci Sports Exerc 2011; 43(12), 2249-2258,3. Wilson GJ, Layman DK, et al. Am J Physiol Endocrinol Metab 2011; 301(6), E1236-1242.4. Pasiakos SM, McClung HL, et al. Am J Clin Nutr 2011; 94(3), 809-818.5. Gingras AA, White PJ, et al. J Physiol 2007; 579(Pt 1), 269-284.6. Olefsky JM and Glass CK. Annu Rev Physiol 2010; 72, 219-246.7. Rivas DA,Yaspelkis BB, et al. J Endocrinol 2009; 202(3), 441-451.8. McDaniel SS, Rensing NR, et al. Epilepsia 2011; 52(3), e7-11.9. Bielohuby M, Sawitzky M, et al. Endocrinology 2011; 152(5), 1948-1960.

# Thread: Golden Tumeric Paste
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/golden-tumeric-paste.11933/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fitness@home — 2014-10-18T19:53:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/golden-tumeric-paste.11933/post-144197>

Not my recipe...it is named Lily's Goldern Tumeric Paste.

I was looking for a way to get more tumeric into my daily life for the anti inflammatory and antiparasitic properties. This fits the bill. I can add to tea or just eat a spoonful of it.

\- 1/2 cup organic tumeric
\- 1 cup water, plus 1/2 cup
\- 1/4 cup raw honey
\- 1/2 cup organic coconut oil
\- 1/2 to 1 tsp black pepper
\- 1-2 tsp combined spices: nutmeg, garam masala, cinnamon, cardomom, etc to taste
\- a pinch of salt

How to make:

Here is where is experimented. I simmered one cup of water with grated ginger root, peppercorns, and cloves for about 10 minutes and then filtered out the water for use in recipe. Added enough water to it to make one cup.

Bring to boil and then add tumeric and whisk. Add spices and continue whisking. Add more water as needed. It will thicken.

Remove from heat after 5-7 minutes. Whisk in coconut oil. Add raw honey and stir until incorporated. I also added a big pinch of red pepper.

Pour into clean, sterile jars and cool. Store in refrigerator.

Now I have enough for at work and home.

I used sterilized canning jars when preparing this and stored them in the fridge. Those three jars lasted me about two months. # Thread: Jack, for calcium help? it's Elaine
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 442
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rain — 2014-10-19T00:22:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-144219>

I apologize in advance for what will surely be a hard to read long email, but believe me it's the condensed version.

I was born by cesarean, to a stressed out mother because she was insane and my father was having an affair.

I nearly died after being born, they didn't know what was wrong with me, i was left to cry in my crib instead of bond with my insanely evil mother

I was sick and abused my whole life, sexually, emotionally, and physically abused at home, and as you may know you carry this 'energy' with you everywhere and the pattern continues forever.

I lost my health utterly by the time I was in my early 20's... I was seeing a doctor who later got kicked out of the medical profession for ruining so many peoples' lives, including mine.

She refused to treat HORRIBLE candida, my hmmhmmm was cut and bleeding and she still wouldn't treat it, she would just say things like 'do you have asthma yet?' and i would have it a few weeks later... the day she said 'ok it's time to test you for MS' i left her office and never went back

I live in canada, and monistat was not available otc at the time, but my brother started going over to border once a week to get me a box, the first box i got i had such a herx reaction i thought i was going to die.

I was so fat and sick by then that i found the 'yeast connection' diet and followed it with weekly monistat until my body gave out, the yeast became monistat resistent, my kidneys gave out, soon i had the worst case of what they called 'Chronic Fatigue Syndrome'... i had lost physics to mini strokes when i was younger and had become a computer programmer by the skin of my teeth on the yeast diet and monistat.

i lost my career, moved out to the coast so the cold didn't hurt so much, and the heat too.... and thought a juice fast would cure me, but no food regime has ever done a thing for my health ever since.

I have no gallbladder, I got most of my stomach cut out, i have IBS, i have horrible PTSD... i was bed ridden for years, diagnosed with CFS, Fibromyalgia, possible MS, Chronic Pain Syndrome that left me screaming in the emergency room every week, now i have either Rheumatoid Arthritis or Lupus, but refused to get tested as i don't want the drugs, oh and i also broke my back when i was 15 years old, a compression fracture of the 3rd and 4th lumbar vertebrae, if you can avoid such a thing i recommend you do.

I don't sleep at night for over 25 yrs, i went on a ketogenic diet 7 - 8 weeks ago and the diet, which by conventional thought should have killed the yeast beast dead, made it RAGE and was so bad i really feel as tho i may die if i don't do something fast.

i found ray peat 3 days ago, and added some sugar slowly, the yeast calmed down some, but the sugar is making me feverish and sick... i'm so sick right now, i'm going back to keto and asking Jack for help...

i am 52 years old now, have been fighting for my health for 30 years, to no avail. you are my last hope, i've tried water fasting because everything i ate, including a raw vegan diet of mostly fruit and carrot juice made me sicker, in fact it's why I ended up with RA or lupus.

on the keto diet i started having weird hypoglycemic comas, and i often feel dizzy and feverish now, and maybe the keto diet made me diabetic, because i didn't crave sugar once and the candida went off the charts, so i can only assume that i had a super high blood glucose level.

i'm fat from psych meds years ago, when i went without sleep for too long and had a nervous breakdown, i was terrified, and got PTSD...

my highest weight was 276 lbs and i'm only 5'5'' i weigh about 200 now, and i'm sick as a dog

i'm terrified because candida stole my life, and now it's back with a vengence.

i need your help, i'm mostly drinking my food, because eating and i don't really get along

being canadian, doctors only prescribe things that will hurt, not things that will help... plus they always say there's nothing wrong with me, of course, just all the mysterious illness catch all phrases.

my mother before me was fat and sick and tried her whole life to get well, but never did and died of pancreatic cancer in the end.

can you tell me what to do? help me, please, for the love of all that's good and holy

i'm sure i've forgotten a lot but i'm in so much pain from typing this that i'll stop here.

I'm really sorry to bother you with this impossible case.... ps, the doctor says there's nothing wrong with my thyroid, but my thyroid actually HURTS, is that normal? i doubt it is

also, all my fat is in my belly from a life of horrible stress from the moment i was conceived.

ok i'll stop now

thank you... help me?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-19T02:07:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-144226>

I'll jump start you.
1\. No gallbladder = high estrogen levels= altered gut flora because of something called FIAF
2\. No gallbladder = low progesterone levels = poor intracellular water = high BUN/creatine ratio
3\. You said your were always fat = makes sense=
Obesity is an inflammatory brain condition that one can get trans-epigenetically from your maternal cell line. Where does the infection come from? The gut flora actually is what causes humans to become fat. It has to due with shear numbers and the species of bacteria in our gut becoming simplified. When this happens to the microbiome, and Vitamin D levels drop. When they drop the gut flora simplifies more and it also decreases the resistance to other infections like candida. When Vitamin D levels lower this causes you to up-regulate carbohydrate metabolism and you begin to crave carbs. This is normal and i mentioned to you on FB why this happens in detail. It is called the Crabtree effect. More on that below. The more carbs you eat the more you deplete iodine and you slow electron chain transport and this diminished your ability to drop of DHA and O2 to your tissues. This makes the brain work not so good. You mentioned on FB you were not particular good and I want you to know why. Iodine works to deliver energy in your CSF around your brain because of the interaction of iodine ions in H+ from water. It is something called the Grotthuss mechanism. Realize that supplemental DHA does NOT FIX this because you need the other parts like iodine and selenium at the same time. You need RAW seafood in a big way ELAINE to feel better.
4\. You may also be one of the people I think supplemental Vitamin D with a light bulb might be a huge great move. You could use oral D3 but with a bad candida issue I would use the skin bulbs or a tanning booth.
5\. I think your need to use the Founier effect. This is when you use Cold Thermogenesis on one part of your body while simultaneously using warm Infrared heat on another. ### Lahelada — 2014-10-19T14:23:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-144268>

Good morning Rain,
Don't think about the impossible,think about the possible,even if it is small.
Is there a way to curtain off the area where you sleep#? If not,can you partition it off a bit with existing
furniture?You could try sleeping with a sleepmask to get at least darkness for the eyes. Could you buy one of them freezable gel eyemasks ,combjne iy with a blackout one over it maybe? Dont know jf that works but you could have a look if you can make them stay together. There is a thread about good sleep and what to do that started recently. One of the things the doc said. Is to breathe jn the cold air from the freezer while yawning.
Food: As much seafood as you can . With you stomach issues could you manage tuna? Can gou get oysters? Oysters would probably help most as they come in a conveniently small package.
Are you mobile enough to go outside and find a park or a patch of grass to enjoy theautumn sun while sityjng barefoot on the ground in cotton clothes? I think as you can only do small thigs you need to do a lot of them,even if they are not 100% optjmal but better than nought,right?
Now about that speed dial, forget about it....
Thinking like that traps you into a dopamine steal syndrome and I woild hazard a guess that you may be deficient in that. You are probably thinkjing "easier said than done" buy think that this is cumulative. Every minute you manage to thjnk "pretty" is a bit of charge jn the ole battery. Music,especially opera fires me up.
There are days when I can almost"eat"
it. Sounds silly? Don't be afraid to be. Hum when you feel despair. It is easy,it
is free and may help.

Hugs from the other end of the continent

### Michele Wilson — 2014-10-19T14:32:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-144269>

I am planning on following Elaine's progress. I see people like her in the ER often, and I know there is no help for them there. It will be interesting to see how her health returns, and as a nurse, sometimes when people are discharged in tears of frustration, I try to "unofficially" point them to a book that I think may help them get to the root of the problem. I am busy on the job and cannot take the time to really explain much, but I know that my reading one book 7 years ago had me eating differently, feeling better, and searching for why that was, and ultimately to more and more understanding as I kept following those rabbit holes. This is amazing how Jack reached out to Elaine and offered some simple solutions to get started right now.
For instance I pointed a woman to a book 2 weeks ago, and she contacted me yesterday and told me her diabetic sugars were better and she lost 7+ lbs already. When she is ready I will point out another book. I did tell her I eat fish once a day and how important that was.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-19T16:00:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-144275>

Elaine embrace your discomfort. it is the force that is going to heal you even though I know that sounds and may feel a bit counterintuitive to you right now. Trust me, I've been there and done it. I know how you're feeling. There is a light and hope. But now you MUST run to it no matter how bad you feel currently. True character is revealed in the choices a human being makes under pressure and you are under that stress now - the greater the pressure, the deeper the revelation becomes. This is what you will find when you get well. Pressure can be a privileges when it is directed. Remember, no pressure, no diamonds in life. As you begin to aim higher in life it seems as though you have more responsibility. This responsibility creates stress and pressure in you and your environment. Without these guiding factors, one can not grow and achieve the greatness you seek. Stay focused and channel your efforts via a "tunneled vision", and you will marvel at the amount of pressure you create in the creation of that vision. It's not what you want or think that you can achieve, it's what you can accomplish that really matters. The more prepared your wisdom is, the less pressure you feel in any task you take. You will become prepared by following those 31 points.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-19T19:36:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-144299>

**Mitochondria swell when calcium is effluxed from a cell causing phosphate to build up in many cells and tissues. In the swollen mitochondria, evident shrinkage may be induced by adding Mg2+ ions in the absence of exogenous ATP. This is why taking Mg2+ supplements without knowing the context is a big error most make. Guys like the Magnesium man don't get this scale of action. You must. When you are suffering in an environment of high non native EMF you must realize the environment must be hacked before your diet or any supplements. This mitochondrial volumetric shrinkage has been determined by spectrophotometric as well as by water content and packed volume measurements for the non believers. See when you know better, you actually do better. I bet you have heard that before on this site.**

### nicld — 2014-10-19T19:44:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-144301>

Welcome Elaine and you are in the right spot.

Jack is right on, embrace the discomfort.

With a small stomach, maybe puree the oysters and drink with seafood broth. I do not have access to fresh oysters so it is canned. I have can to a can and a half every morning for me along with salmon and it has made a huge difference in the mental clarity. Check out Inger's fish head smoothies. I destroyed my health by working nights and going back to college. I lost alot of my smarts but it is coming back.

Toss a towel over the alarm clock. That's what I do with the one of the hubbies side. I have a battery operated one emits no light. Maybe look into getting one of those for him. I have this mask and it works pretty good. [http://www.amazon.com/dp/B000CCI4YU/ref=sr\_ph?ie=UTF8&qid=1413735709&sr=1&keywords=sleep+mask](http://www.amazon.com/dp/B000CCI4YU/ref=sr_ph?ie=UTF8&qid=1413735709&sr=1&keywords=sleep+mask)

Also get a pair of these ASAP.
[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000NP6NG2/ref=ox\_sc\_sfl\_title\_5?ie=UTF8&psc=1&smid=ATVPDKIKX0DER](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000NP6NG2/ref=ox_sc_sfl_title_5?ie=UTF8&psc=1&smid=ATVPDKIKX0DER) These have made the biggest difference for me in helping get the sleep better. I used to get tired, crawl in bed, lay there for an hour or two, sleep maybe one cycle, wake back up for a couple of more hours and if I was lucky, get one more cycle in before needing to get up for work and felt like crap. Now, I put these on as soon as it get dark, and can feel the melatonin increase (I get cold when that does) and can fall asleep fairly soon and the best part, stay asleep.

Oh and i think CT rocks and LOVE my deep CT. I use the snow to get my baths really really cold in the winter.

There is hope but it will take alot of work and maybe some pretty hard decisions.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-19T20:16:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-144306>

[Candida proteins resemble gluten](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=hwp1+protein+gluten) proteins so they all cause a leaky gut. A leaky gut is on that leaks too much light from the microbiome to your enterocytes. This light destroys the amount of Vitamin D in enterocytes. This decreases the activation of the immune system in the GALT which is right behind the gut lining. This is why some people who have tried the recent paleo craze of resistant starch have contacted me about their recent spikes in their autoimmune conditions. CONTEXT IS KEY and I pointed that out in the January 2014 webinar on my RS bio hack. Once an immune reaction against candida commences, patients will generally develop frank celiac or severe gluten intolerance/hypersensitivity. Celiac disease can be created by this mechanism using mis folded proteins that alter the polarized light signal to T regulator cells if certain HLA DQ2/8 immune cells are present. The lower your vitamin D level is the more likely they will be present. The immune system will begin to slowly attack foods with wheat, barley, rye and oats with a vigorously because these contain or have proteins antigens that simulate gluten when macrophages present the antigen to the T regulator cells. This causes a loss of iodine and electrons in proteins making them more hydrophobic and less hydrophilic meaning theire will be less H+ in the enterocytes and the GALT in which to turn of the switch. This has massive effects on T4 and T3 production in the gut too. Stopping gluten in foods helps........but it is only a very small part of the Rx for fixing this night mare. I personally believe this is how Hashimoto's has exploded in our modern world.
