**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-144327>

I don't think my whole house is grounded but I have a personal grounding strap hooked up now. I have a spot in my basement with open ground (I have an old old house). So I pounded a 4 foot copper grounding rod it. Attached a coated copper wire to it and ran it up the stairs. To this, I have another copper wire attached and have attached a grounding wrist strap to my arm. Oh, I also have a pretty inexpensive volt meter that I am using to test everything, borrowed it from work since it has been sitting at my desk for a couple of years.

### Justfourme — 2014-10-20T01:56:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-144334>

rain - when I read through your posts, I am amazed by your strength and determination. Dr Kruse has given you a very precious gift, read and re-read often.
As Caroline suggested, a journal is a good idea, not only for communication with others, but you can reference back to it to remind yourself of the milestones you have already achieved.
Most of us here are not afraid to think outside of the box, you need to challenge yourself to do the same.
You have an RV you can no longer use, can you sell or rent it out? Could be an income stream that you could use to buy a different RV, away from EMF, out by a nice cold stream in the woods...
No tub for CT....and Jack recommends a hot tub with cold air....but you have a shower - so buy a big plastic tote that will fit in the shower, fill with warm/hot water and stand or kneel in that with ice packs on your top half. Zip lock baggies tied on with towels would be a cheap ice vest.
Make sure along with the seafood, you are eating the best quality organic food you can. Yes, organic can be expensive so spend $ wisely. Start with organic berries, apples and lettuce.
And bone broth - very healing and would be easy on your stomach.
Remember you didn't get this way overnight, so you won't be fixed overnight. Baby steps for now.
And as far as your hubby - he has stuck with you thru all the ups and downs. He will be amazed at how you will be able to turn things around.
We are all here with different challenges and will be here to support and guide.
Welcome!!

### nonchalant — 2014-10-20T05:40:01+0300

Welcome, Elaine, glad to have you here with us.
I think I'm going to have to print out this thread to reread again. Awesome. Thanks so much for prompting this response from Dr. K. I think a lot of us can relate to various parts of your history.

His podcasts are an easier way to access the information. Please ask if you have more questions. My husband was also skeptical of many of Dr. K's protocols, but he has been amazed at how CT baths help my hormones. And recently he has used early morning sunshine to cure an illness he was experiencing. I think once you begin to see some benefits, your husband might be more receptive.

Just be aware, the benefits you first see might not be with what you see as your worst problem. Your body will work on its own priorities first. It is working hard to keep you alive, so give it some slack. Remember that everyone is in the absolute best health, given their situations.

### Penny — 2014-10-20T07:47:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-144395>

Hi Elaine

I have a few ideas on your digestion... what a surprise... you can't get energy from food you can't break down... so, in the beginning, you should take digestive enzymes plus ox bile plus betaine HCL to help you absorb the food so it turns into electron. The product is called Now Foods digestive enzymes and it's $13.00...

[http://www.amazon.com/NOW-Foods-Sup...6&sr=8-1&keywords=now+foods+digestive+enzymes](http://www.amazon.com/NOW-Foods-Super-Enzymes-Capsules/dp/B0013OXKHC/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1413775926&sr=8-1&keywords=now+foods+digestive+enzymes)

The next issue is the gut lining - you need zinc, iodine, d and B1 - zinc is a biggie but in order to break down zinc you need stomach acid - in order to have stomach acid, you need zinc - thus the issue is solved if you just take zinc with some betaine HCL - betaine HCL is supplemental stomach acid -

You can also take zinc bound to carnosine to heal the gut:
[http://www.amazon.com/Doctors-Best-...76148&sr=8-1&keywords=pepzin+gi+doctor's+best](http://www.amazon.com/Doctors-Best-PepZin-Veggie-120-Count/dp/B0035BYOSA/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1413776148&sr=8-1&keywords=pepzin+gi+doctor%27s+best)

Ox bile is also critical in your case as you have no gall bladder to store bile - which is used to digest fat...

Just curious, how is threelac working out for you?

I'm a huge fan of Saccharomyces boulardii probiotic for candida and Thorne research has a great product calle Sf 722:

"This protocol is from Stephen Harrod Buhner's book "Healing Lyme: Natural Healing and Prevention of Lyme Borreliosis and It's Coinfections". Has anyone tried it?

To Kill Candida (both acids are stongly inhibitive):
Capryl (SolarRay Brand) 2163 mg. Caprylic acid 2 caps 3X
Undecenioc acid (Thorne Research Formula SF722) 50 mg
2 caps 3X

To Enhance Bowel/System Normalization:
Thymic Formula (Preventative Theraputics) 2 tabs 3X
Molybdenum picolinate 1,000 mg. 1X

To Restore Bowel Flora:
PB8 (probiotic acidophilius caps) 2 capsules per day
Saccharomyces boulardii 1 capsule 3X per day"

I've been having some success with the Garden of Life probiotics "Primal Defense Ultra" - what I like about it is it contains one non-spore forming soil based organism bacillus subtillus - spore forming probiotics freak me out - it also contains s. Boulardi and l. Plantarum - if you take this probiotic and toss down an l. reuteri, you pretty much have it all covered...

For the free and easy candida kill, make a tea with spices - fresh is best but even the dry stuff works great: oregano, rosemary, bay leaf, garlic, thyme (great for h. pylori), turmeric (great inflammatory modulator - use it with black pepper and ginger), ginger, cinnamon, cloves (great for parasites too) -

You might also want to throw some baking soda (1/4 tsp) in your drinking water to alkalize yourself - we just buy alkaline water - I personally know someone who cured his eczema by drinking alkaline water... I would really recommend green smoothies for that effect not to mention all the electrons you get from raw food... plus the micronutrients - make them organic - try the farmers market - becaue you don't want a pesticide factory in your gut along with the rest of your ills...

A cheap way to kill h. pylori - you might have that too - is black cumin seed oil - 2 pills twice a day - it's as effective as the triple therapy - you want to cover all bases - and all bugs that might be killing you as fast and cheap as possible:
[http://www.amazon.com/Life-Extensio...13777793&sr=8-2&keywords=black+cumin+seed+oil](http://www.amazon.com/Life-Extension-Black-Cumin-Softgels/dp/B009E7SO1M/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1413777793&sr=8-2&keywords=black+cumin+seed+oil)

Getting rid of the alchohol (acetyl aldehyde) the candida is producing is critical to feeling better - this is where molybdenum and B5 come into play:

[http://lauraslymerecovery.wordpress.com/2013/07/22/the-candida-and-acetaldehyde-connection/](http://lauraslymerecovery.wordpress.com/2013/07/22/the-candida-and-acetaldehyde-connection/)

Dr. Atkins actually was a huge fan of molybdenum and b5 for kids with ADHD - it's water soluble so maybe start with 100 mg a few times a day up to 1000 mg on the molybdenum per day - and some pantothenic acid and pantathine (in case you have conversion issues) -

You also might try a product called Fibro Response - it gets rid of metals, modulates your immune system and is a great synergistic product that gives you energy - it's for fibromyalgia - but if you're in pain, it might help - it has malic acid which gets rid of aluminum and helps with the krebs cycle - which is huge in ill people IMHO...

For hypoxia, we have had good luck with rhodiola, astragulus and maca -

Here is an acid/alkaline food chart:
[http://nancymullanmd.com/Acid\_Alkaline\_Chart.htm](http://nancymullanmd.com/Acid_Alkaline_Chart.htm)

There is an Italian oncologist Tullio Simoncini injecting cancerous tumors with baking soda and killing them - when voltage goes low, oxygen drops, acidity rises and bugs think "roadkill" - so maybe if you alkalize you can improve your electron flow... and change the form your pathogens take...

The redox RX is fabulous :

[http://jackkruse.com/redox-rx/](http://jackkruse.com/redox-rx/)

Maybe read by a red incandescant lamp (red bulbs are $3.00) and put ice packs on your body when you go to bed? It helps me sleep - an eye mask is also good -

Soaking in Epsom Salts is a fabulous/cheap way to detoxify and get magnesium - candida generates a lot of toxins...

I really feel for you - I can't even begin to comprehend what pain you have endured - your husband probably cares about you but is overwhelmed by the scope of your issues - pick the biggest issue that you can do something about and fix that first - having a plan also helps - hope I helped a little... take care and welcome

Penny

Boulardi and l. Plantarum - if you take this probiotic and toss down an l. reuteri, you pretty much have it all covered...
>
> For the free and easy candida kill, make a tea with spices - fresh is best but even the dry stuff works great: oregano, rosemary, bay leaf, garlic, thyme (great for h. pylori), turmeric (great inflammatory modulator - use it with black pepper and ginger), ginger, cinnamon, cloves (great for parasites too) -
>
> You might also want to throw some baking soda (1/4 tsp) in your drinking water to alkalize yourself - we just buy alkaline water - I personally know someone who cured his eczema by drinking alkaline water... I would really recommend green smoothies for that effect not to mention all the electrons you get from raw food... plus the micronutrients - make them organic - try the farmers market - becaue you don't want a pesticide factory in your gut along with the rest of your ills...
>
> A cheap way to kill h. pylori - you might have that too - is black cumin seed oil - 2 pills twice a day - it's as effective as the triple therapy - you want to cover all bases - and all bugs that might be killing you as fast and cheap as possible:
> [http://www.amazon.com/Life-Extension-Black-Cumin-Softgels/dp/B009E7SO1M/ref=sr\_1\_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1413777793&sr=8-2&keywords=black cumin seed oil](http://www.amazon.com/Life-Extension-Black-Cumin-Softgels/dp/B009E7SO1M/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1413777793&sr=8-2&keywords=black+cumin+seed+oil)
>

>

>
> Dr. Atkins actually was a huge fan of molybdenum and b5 for kids with ADHD - it's water soluble so maybe start with 100 mg a few times a day up to 1000 mg on the molybdenum per day - and some pantothenic acid and pantathine (in case you have conversion issues) -
>
> You also might try a product called Fibro Response - it gets rid of metals, modulates your immune system and is a great synergistic product that gives you energy - it's for fibromyalgia - but if you're in pain, it might help - it has malic acid which gets rid of aluminum and helps with the krebs cycle - which is huge in ill people IMHO...

> **Why should I avoid aerobic exercise during the reset?**
> This will require some explanation. If you’re LR, you are a sugar burner, not a fat burner. That means you can not do glycolytic exercise until your muscles and lever become completely LS as well. Your brain being LS is not enough. If your muscles are still LR, they can’t properly use the glucose or the fat delivered to them. This is detailed in the post [Why is Oprah Still Obese? Leptin Part 3](http://jackkruse.com/why-is-oprah-still-obese-leptin-part-3/).
> Another to avoid aerobic exercise at this stage is because the AMPk pathways are not working well yet. (This will be the focus of an upcoming blog post.) AMPk is stimulated by re-teaching your muscles and liver how to deplete glycogen and metabolically respond to hypoxia and cellular stressors. Any time metabolism is stressed, AMPk should respond in kind with big results. In LR, it does not work well at all. This is why body composition is trashed in LR states. If you do aerobic exercise when you are still leptin resistant, you send signals to the mitochondria that conditions are really bad. Instead of making new mitochondria to help out, you wind up killing your cells via apoptosis. New stems cells are recruited to replace the suicidal cells. Sounds good? The problem is those stem cells were going to help extend your life span, and, well, you just shortened it! This is why NFL players, bodybuilders, and marathoners don’t live long as group, and why they get many disease early, such as heart attacks, Alzheimers, and cancer. Not good. Longevity always mandates we protect our stem cells. We only want to use them when we really need them. If you use them early in life, you’re subtracting years from the end of your life.

[http://jackkruse.com/the-leptin-rx-faqs/](http://jackkruse.com/the-leptin-rx-faqs/)

### Da-mo — 2014-10-24T13:28:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-144941>

Suflur, interesting . . . have you seen the tensegrity 7 blog yet?

Bear with me while I talk about horses for a bit but it does relate to both suflur and the tensegrity 7 blogpost . . . .

Horses get something called mud fever if on their legs and people call it sunburn if on their noses. It usually occurs on white fur or skin where the blood comes close to the surface. I put flowers of sulfur on my horse when he gets it on his legs and it clears right up. Others put zinc on the noses that get "sunburned" but it doesnt seem to work as well as the sulfur does on their legs. I think all the zinc does is act as a sunblock whereas the sulfur seems to be doing something much more effective.

Now, in relation to the blogpost . . . . the theory is that at certain times of year certain grasses the horses eat are high in photodynamic pigments (e.g. high powered high sugar white clover intended for beef weight gain and high milk production). These pigments flouresce when carried in the blood close to the skin surface where they are "powered up" by sunlight - result: horse gets a radiation burn from the inside.

I wonder if there are parallels with sunburn in humans given the latest blogpost when diet doesnt match for sunlight given horses are mammals too . . . . . . . . . .

Also, see the dark circles under eyes post for seemingly related stuff . . . . [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...to-daily-life-dark-circles.11962/#post-144872](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/quantum-aspect-to-daily-life-dark-circles.11962/#post-144872)

I've nothing to offer on the itching - just throwing ideas at you since you mentioned sulfur and it seems to be tied to skin, sunlight, vitamin D etc.

Perhaps a mixture of topical sulfur along with morning sun exposure? (which you should be doing anyway I think)

Also see [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...cium-channel-blocker.11956/page-2#post-144817](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/sulfated-vitamin-d3-is-your-major-calcium-channel-blocker.11956/page-2#post-144817) in conjunction with the tensegrity 7 blogpost to tie in how it may offer some protection against nnEMF (as calcium channel blocker)

### Justfourme — 2014-10-25T14:34:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-145066>

On those cravings - could be that candida raising it's ugly head. It will try to trick you into eating sugary things to stay alive. Don't let it win.
A thought on the rash - I vote that your body is detoxing. Your skin is your biggest organ. It's either a reaction or allergy to something. Try to eliminate all processed foods, have you changed laundry soap?
Had a lady I know have something similar and they finally linked it to celiac, an autoimmune reaction and something to do with the liver. She was covered with itchy rash head to toe. She is now fine.

### Martin — 2014-10-25T15:37:14+0300

Rain! No teasing for the noob. I know, it's a tough concept to (_ahem)_ swallow, but when you're up against the wall like most of us, we must get out of our comfort zone and try new things. Like dipping our bods into cold-ass water, wearing funny colored glasses at night, and eating like a shark. We humans have forgotten what it takes to be _truly_ human.

Try sneaking up on a mackerel. Observe the mackerel. Touch the mackerel. Contemplate the mackerel. Then fillet and pan-fry the sucker. Drizzle lemon juice and eat. See food, eat food.

All silliness aside, endeavor to change your comfort zone and become more ancestral, but add modern measures to mitigate this nn-EMF whirlwind we are in, ay.

NSH

### Penny — 2014-10-26T05:26:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-145191>

Hey darlin'... my advice is toss the threelac... or at least take a break from it... from a reviewer on Amazon with a burning rash from threelac... see a doctor - pronto!

1.0 out of 5 stars **Beware of ThreeLac and do thorough research before trying**, January 12, 2012
By
[Brad Southard](http://www.amazon.com/gp/pdp/profile/AASKHGT3ECBKU/ref=cm_cr_pr_pdp/175-5491908-7031623) (Irvine, CA United States) - [See all my reviews](http://www.amazon.com/gp/cdp/member-reviews/AASKHGT3ECBKU/ref=cm_cr_pr_auth_rev/175-5491908-7031623?ie=UTF8&sort_by=MostRecentReview)

**Verified Purchase**([What's this?](http://www.amazon.com/gp/community-help/amazon-verified-purchase/175-5491908-7031623))
**This review is from: Threelac Probiotic Dietary Supplement, Natural Lemon Flavor, Includes 60 .053-Ounce Packets (Health and Beauty)**
I just stopped taking ThreeLac because I developed a severe skin infection with a burning rash. At first I did not connect the ThreeLac to the rash. After several days it became worse and I saw a doctor who prescribed anti-biotics for the skin infection. I stopped taking the ThreeLac since I was now taking anti-biotics and decided to do an internet search for other probiotics that I could take while on the antibiotic. I came across a Holistic health care provider's website where she described her reaction to ThreeLac and many other people left comments about their experiences...most negative. I was surprised since I had read so many positive reviews and I thought that probiotics could do no harm - they would either help or do nothing so I thought. After reading through those experiences I felt weak and panicky because I realized that all my current symptoms were due to the release of a pathogenic bacteria into my bloodstream which is the last ingredient in ThreeLac: enterococcus faecalis. It is a bacteria that normally resides in our intestines, but if you have too much, of have leaky gut syndrome (as most Candida sufferers do), it can enter you bloodstream and cause all kinds of infections. Many people reported getting urinary tract infections which led to kidney infections, and most people reported feeling weak, tired, dizzy, and having other related problems. I too feel sick and weak and horrible with this rash - all my symptoms meet the those of an infection with enterococcus faecalis.

I am not sure if ThreeLac is a complete scam or just a very dangerous "nutritional" supplement that affects people in extreme ways - either very good or very bad. Either way, please do your research before trying it because it's better to be safe than sorry. I am sorry. I believed all the positive reviews and let down my "scam sniffing" guard because I did not think a pro-biotic could cause me harm. I have read that Australia and Canada do not allow this bacteria into food products because it is extremely resistant to antibiotics and pathogenic to some people. Most ironic that the packages say "Pure Natural Food" when it feels like i just unknowingly poisoned myself and may have to spend lots of money getting treatment since I am uninsured and may be waiting too long get proper medical help. Please do not give this to your children without trying it for yourself first. I read some sad experiences of well-intentioned parents giving it to kids with awful side effects.

I am heading back to the tried-and-true natural candida remedies that I used over ten years ago and worked: Caprylic acid, GOOD probiotics (that are legal in other countries with better oversight than our FDA), and and a Candida diet.

From Penny:

You need to get to a doctor pronto and get some vancomycin - if you add ginger juice when you take the vancomycin, it will help the resistance factor of the microbe to the vancomycin... this is from Steven Buhner's Herbal Antibiotics book... he gives a list of tinctures you could use, but screw that, get to a doctor - this bug can affect your heart...

Do not \*ever\* use Tylenide as it can deplete your glutathione... your body's main antioxidant - baking soda or calamine lotion might be helpful - any kind of clay might help also - according to the reviewer, you might go see your doctor and get some antibiotics...

I know it costs $100.00 but if you gave a stool sample to American Gut, they would give you the DNA of your gut flora and you could see what is actually killing you - at least bacteria-wise... the little buggers never travel alone...

[http://americangut.org/](http://americangut.org/)

But please, take the package of threelac to a doctor and have him test your blood for e. faecalis... this is quite serious... and let me know how you fared...

Hello, first time i speak in this thread...

What i see is quite simple, i like doing it simple because we often complicate things up.

If you start to do changes that goes toward a natural state, stepping back, falling and getting bruises can be good for you. When you take a couple of steps back you can get on a better track that gives you health once again.

Often i see in this forum, exactly what you feel and senses and i think like this:

Getting natural energy back into our systems starts with a reversal, we start doing things to kick systems into function once again. We get cold, we get dark and we try to sleep better but, this is big for me... We forget that when the unit you starts to work again we need the real stuff, the dirty, smelly, wet and wonderful nature. We must get out, out into the woods, the oceans, the icy and dark world that we all share.

Modern human is like fish pulled from sea, left on beach, slowly drying up and dying. We need to get juicy again!

My own rule is and has been for all of my life... Any natural light is good light, it does not matter if it is december in my place with just three hours of light, i get it, right up my face and body. Just because we think clouds cover the sun, photons reach us and tells us what season it is, this is why simulating nature is so freaking hard to do.

I think you should share my number one, like many others do here, but we tend to get stuck in our minds from time to time... GET OUT!

Many that i meet, they are suffering big time, starting when the fall began. Modern world is cashing in, we pay the dept with health for the gain of comfort. My own town is filled with flu, headaches and zombie like humans. This has been going on for a long time but it has bloomed, like i said this fall.

Often we tend to fall into traces of our past, this applies to many, therefor food is always going to be the ace of spades. What we must understand is often, the thing that makes one ill, perhaps is not the reason. Many things depend on how the components we are built upon rhyme.

Dig deep into the world of physics instead of looking at diets and styles, many have pounded that theory for ages and look where we are today, slipping and sliding and experts do not understand.

The smallest thing can and will always be able to sink a great ship. Just because it is so small, you can not see it does not mean, you are safe. This is why we all have different states every single day. One word can turn you into a monster and a single faint of smell can make you cry like a baby. Scale this to living and we will find what each of us is missing not what others say you do.

When we live in times where technology is competing for those dear photons, electrons and the other parts we have not found, we have a problem... We, humans are not that many compared to those theives, therefor eliminate them as much as possible and reload yourself with nature, stand above gadgets and look out from mountain tops, rule the world.

i have ZERO other ways to change my environment, the wifi i can't turn off, the smart meters i can't get away from, the EMF of the city that also makes me cry like a baby, i have no escape from

food is not the reason i'm ill, i have tried every kind of eating on the planet, only to get sicker and sicker

Prana, thank you for understanding that i have a bigger problem here, this forum is nice isn't it? but i'm not in a good mood right now that's for sure...

That means that smell is far more important than most think.

That means hearing is vital to health.

That means touch is how we connect.

The list goes on and food, yes, way down... I said before that we perhaps are thinking and reading to much, yes i still do because these ways of describing things is to dumb, low bandwith. Another things is also, healing is for me stealing. When you start to add energy into a system that is based on small tiny semiconductors (living things) actions do start. A faint step into the right way cause tsunamis in our bodies, system drained is speeding up, filling the quota. I think we are based upon laws of LIFO (last in first out). Can we change our brain to adapt to these harsh conditions, i think we done it tons of times. That is why i always say, find a way to get out. ### nicld — 2014-10-28T18:12:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-145429>

I am with you about the MHS Rain, just not ready to go there plus not even sure where to get some.

I think the reason why food is suggested first is that it is the easiest thing for people to change and to control. Changing what one is eating is way easier then someone giving up modern conveniences that make life more comfortable.

I was like that and to some point still am dealing with food issues and figured food can fix anything, it's all about the food. It is what has been crammed down our throats for so long, eat this, not that, this will get you better, this is the next miracle food, this will kill you and for years I bought into that too.

_**Food can hurt and it can help but it cannot totally fix.**_

I am not about to quit my job and go live in a cave somewhere with only natural fire to heat and see by. What I am trying to figure it is the best way to reduce my exposures to stuff that is bad for my health and then figure out what I need to do to help the body deal with what I am exposed too. Right now, that is a Magnetico pad, grounding when I can, lots of cold CT, limiting my use of electronics, keeping the house cool, trying to keep the lights as low as possible and if they are on at night, covering up and wearing blueblockers, no wi-fi in the house, trying to get as much sunlight but when I can't using a sun box light. Plus I also live in the country with lots of pine trees around. I do have the luxury of having some funds to eat more seafood as it is not a staple in my area and some supplements that may help but not fix. Is it enough, probably not but I will keep trying to get it better and I do see that I am slowly getting better.

As for you environment, not sure what to tell you. Are you able to get a new grounding wire and have yourself grounding all the time? I think prAna is on to something with the sun causing issues.

Wishing you all the best as you definitely have some greater challenges that you are working with but you can do it.

Oh and I fully get the whole blog thing. 10 years ago I would have understood everything better and it is hard to realize that but it is slowly coming back and it may for you too.

### Linz — 2014-10-29T11:29:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-145534>

Elaine, sorry you are feeling so bad and I don't want to add to information overload but I think more context might help some of the things you are struggling with. Keep taking on the protocols you can cope with at the moment but remember it can take up to two years to become an efficient fat burner so it's not surprising that going headlong into ketosis can stir some interesting discomfort. Three things you could consider.
1\. Keep adding plenty of the right fats in the right season whatever diet choices you make (as well as fish and seaweeds for minerals) so that your mitochondria start adapting, and if you can reduce the starchy veg content in winter do it slowly
2\. Be aware that in nature ketosis is a winter state and really needs cold to work as it should. Cold switches us from summer light to winter temperature regulation (many of us in trouble here!!!)
3\. Mitochondrial supplements might help you adapt and also give some protection from emf - ubiquinol, mg, acetyl-l-carnitine, d-ribose.

Healing is a peculiar journey. Things you never knew you had disappear and those you are desperate to cure seem determined to turn into raging monsters. I saw the first real improvement by looking back and realising that many of my most acute episodes were followed by real lasting improvements and often the monsters were just having a last frightening fling.

Don't let the things you can't contemplate now ruin your cortisol/melatonin special relationship. Just keep them somewhere for another day. You may feel your real brain has fallen apart but your posts here seem incredibly intelligent and intuitive.

The medical community is actually UN-helpful to us ...and it’s not their fault either. We are victims and Dr K is a true healer in helping us -freely. Still in all, it’s our responsibility to do the work for ourselves.

FWIW: I used to race motocross - broke bones and picked the bike up & kept riding, worked construction for many years, and I’m a weight-lifter …and what he has given you made the water come to my eyes. He’s a pretty smart guy and knows full well he could cash-in huge on his revelations. And he gives it to us freely.

I bet after you heal you can pay it forward amazingly.

In point 15 there may be a typo; “When the proteins mis fold you **can** signal things properly in your brain and this makes you feel like you are mentally slow and mentally ill and depressed.” I think the word “can” should be “can’t”, but he would have to confirm that. I’m a noob here.

If it were me, I’d follow Dr K’s advice to a tee …and I’m skeptical to the point of cynicism! I’ve started his protocols already …and I had to study ketogenisis for many months before I was convinced. PubMed is my friend

Now I study what Dr. K is teaching, checking the researchers and Docs he cites, and reading the books he recommends. I’m in a hurry because my lipid panels are very bad and my Dr here insists I get on statins – not good.

This community seems very supportive. I’ve already had many light-bulb moments. I hope you find strength here and heal up over time.

IMHO: This place, and this moment in time, is going to be viewed as damn-near magic …when time reports the truth in retrospect.

### louise — 2014-11-02T22:23:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-145967>

hello Elaine,
no don't give up---I don't think you will anyway
you said that now you are suffering horribly, sobbing and panicking----this is awful and I completely empathise this with you Elaine-----I was like this for many years (16 yrs ago) I know 16yrs ago is a long time but sometimes it feels like yesterday---during this time I was suicidal and tried many times to take my own life but found myself in some poisons unit the following day------it took me the best part of 5 years to conquer the anxiety and it still creeps up on me occasionally......
you can make it Elaine, and I agree that you may not need any diet etc but I know that for me personally I would never have climbed out of my dark dark hole if I had not changed the way I think and by that I specifically mean meditation
something HAD to change in my life and I can see you are open to all sorts of healing techniques
we are all different and what works for some may not for others
I wanted to express this differently and its come out all wrong----I prefer to write without thinking first as it feels more relaxed and from the heart so I suppose what im saying is that I got myself some meditation books and went to a chi kung class which I was able to go to for free as I was in a drug rehab so im fully aware that doing stuff when you are in a safe institution is very different and I might not have made the effort otherwise
it was a slow progress tho Elaine and many types of meditation were tried but I had horrible "monkey mind" and anxiety etc-----alternate nostril breathing DOES work for anxiety---I had been a massive benzo addict and the panic, paranoia and anxiety were killing me-----along with my negative thoughts
mindfulness also helps--A LOT
I don't know what else to say Elaine but I do understand the despair and I do understand that you can have information overload at times like this-----if I lived by you I would come over everyday and MAKE you practise chi kung and do lots of mindfulness-----chi kung is done on grass barefoot and you literally visualize the earth energy (electrons) passing into your body and you can feel the magnetic field around you and between your hands----its immense
thinking back now I realise that when I slacked off from meditation then that is when things start to slide again
I turned my health and life and mental state around all those years ago without any special diet or having to go and live in a cave etc----all I did was changed my thoughts from negative to positive altho it was waaaaay harder than it sounds when I just write it like that
Elaine you will be ok and this time will pass....you are really strong....that comes across in your posts......keep coming to this forum Elaine, you will find some answers, I know you will...we all find our way

### nicld — 2014-11-02T22:56:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-145970>

Here is my take on what I got from his videos and alot of the information here.

Our bodies are like a house. Some people have houses that are ok but has somethings that need fixing and others have houses that are close to being condemned. Electrons are like extra money that can be used to update the house. Sometimes things have to be tore down and look worse before it can be fixed (should have seen our bathroom before and during remodel). Some people are able to afford to get the best material and the best people who can get it done quickly and some people need to get average stuff, do some of the work them selves and take way more time to get it done (me me me). To me this is the difference in food in my mind, fresh oysters and seafood versus canned and frozen and red meat. Sometimes when there is a so much extra money (electrons) work begins on tearing everything down and nothing gets completed. Then there are time when money dries up (electrons) and workers go on strike or one runs out of building materials.

This is how I think a journey to optimal is. I was pretty dilapidated a 16 months ago and during the "remodel" things got worse. Still working on remodeling and it is going slowly. Do I have a nice new shiney house with everything working the way it should, nope. But what I do have is plumbing that is working better  but the electrical still needs work. Will I ever be perfect, probably not but I know that I will be better then I was.

I really don't have any insight into when things might get better for you but all I can attest to is that it will take time.

Do you have a body voltage meter to see where you are at?

### Da-mo — 2014-11-03T11:08:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-146014>

Just want to clarify - JK did NOT say hot and cold showers, he said Fournier effect which is a hell of a lot easier and he also said this would help alleviate other things quicker . . . . . . and that includes your mitochondria's ability to handle fat.

> 5\. I think your need to use the Founier effect. **This is when you use Cold Thermogenesis on one part of your body while simultaneously using warm Infrared heat on another. Since you live in Canada this will be easy to do. Get naked in a warm spring or hot tub and at 100-104 degrees and then leave your top half out to the environmental cold while drinking cold water. This will improve the mitochondrial function in your brain and heart and help you feel better.**
> 6\. The stressors in your life constantly destroy the amount of DHA in your mitochondria and tissues naturally, so the more stress the more seafood you will need. You said you were quite stressed so one meal a day needs to be seafood. Excessive blue light will also destroy it so on FB you said you felt great with a fire. Use the campfire a lot at night. Great way to get IR heat.
> 7\. There is a particular flora that produces adiposity and obesity in humans. These bacteria make something called FIAF ([Fasting induced adipose factor](http://gordonlab.wustl.edu/PublicationPDFs/344_BackhedPNAS04.pdf)) that control this process. This factor blocks lipoprotein lipase (LPL) in fat cells. LPL allows us to convert dietary Free Fatty Acids carried in lipoproteins into neutral fats that are stored in adipocytes. This is why you have always been heavy. **The Fournier effect will help you mobilize that fat.**
> 8\. **Your mitochondria wont be ready for these fats right away but if you keep up the Fournier effect they will rather quickly.**

ain't no snake oil on this site . . . and the science is self evident whether you want to believe it or not so it aint no faith healing neither

### Linz — 2014-11-03T17:16:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-146035>

Elaine,
It's a really good start that you know it was estrogen that caused your rash and mood changes, and that you have realised how powerful the effect of cold is.

At the beginning of the thread, 'How was your ct today?' many described similar effects after diving into too much too soon. Da-mo emphasised a gentler way above that will let you adapt to fat burning (which also needs a more gradual start). Many started by just dipping their face in cold water every day to initiate the dive reflex. Also a useful gauge of response to more intense ct. Headaches/brain freeze/hives are temporary response to estrogen or omega 6:3 imbalance and a warning to go gradually.

You collect electrons from natural grounding. Water is the best conductor. The ocean is best, then natural water source or wet grass, but any natural ground will help too or even sit in a tree.
Energy dense food is an important source of electrons too and fish is the best especially raw ( which also promotes estriol in the gut - the safest kind of estrogen). Adding sea vegetables ensures all the right minerals - iodine/selenium etc in a safe form to help you use the energy.
I think drinking good quality cold water and keeping light as natural as possible allows us to rehydrate and use the electrons efficiently ( but I don't know anything about using the light box where you live). As Kovita said healing is all about using nature the right way, and the protocols here may accelerate nature for us but like supplements, too much at the wrong time or place can hurt but usually the only way to find your own limit is to jump in. If you ask for advice here first many will help so you are more aware of the risks or likely discomfort which should help you make sense of your own situation.

Take care

### Da-mo — 2014-11-07T03:49:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-146410>

Maybe its too late, but just in case it isnt . . . . . .

Do what you can do and hopefully those little things will help you build enough strength to do the things you think you cant do.

I'll explain the above in my own personal terms - nowhere near what you are going through but structurally similar.

My biggest single health problem is smoking tobacco. Presently, if I stop, I get horrible mood swings and make really bad health decisions due to low brain dopamine levels. I know what I should do but it all goes out the window when my dopamine levels drop. .

Something I learned when coaching football - when players are under pressure they will revert to their habits. Teach them good habits and they will perform even better under pressure - teach them bad habits and they will fall apart. The most important of these, even more than skills and physical ability, is mindset and attitude.

So for now I'm working on all the easy things and forming good habits to get me to a place where I can tackle the big one and respond to the pressure in a positive way.

How do you eat an elephant? One bite at a time.
Something I used to drill into the players - no-one has beaten you until you have chosen to give up.

### kovita — 2014-11-07T13:42:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-146425>

Elaine, i will be very rude now and I do it only because I relly want to help you in some way and I wish for you only the best. When I read your posts, I can see there a lot of very familiar things to me. I have lived most of my life in some sort of mist, where evrything was somehow meant to hurt me and I was missing everything good in life because of the way I have (unwillingly) chosen to perceive and live the life. I was continuously after something, being very agressive to myself and I guess to others as well. It was a real inferno for me and i am only able to see it now, when I can observe it from a distance. Having my own kids did not bring any change, sadly. When i got sick I became a selfish, angry, hysterical and sad person who hated evrything and everyone, even my own kids sometimes, when it went to my mind that I gave my own health for their lifes. There was always someone to be blamed for my personal disaster, it was comfortable and easy and most importnatly it diverted me from what could be truth or solutions. After giving up on doctors, i threw myself into supplements and herbs etc. blindly. without any change at all. than. i started to read this blog and forum, just reading no participation, and the change in my interno slowly started.... I cried a lot when I realized, that my disease is nothing more or nothing less than the true reflexion of who I have been. the way I behaved towards myself (since childhood or as long as I remember) was suddenly just the same as my disease. I blamed my father, my mother everyone for a long long time for who I am till all these thoughts became part of my physical reality. i started to "invest" in my thoughts, love, the happiness and laughs of my family and it works just like a magic. It sounds like the biggest bullshit ever, doesn't it? And yet it is the most powerfull supplement for my physical disease I have found so far. Anger was my personal disease for a long time.

### NeilBB — 2014-11-07T15:04:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-146429>

Freewill gives no one the power to instantaneously correct health, but it gives everyone the power to instantly alter life's direction.

Depending on one's starting point, it might take a long time with excellent navigation to eventually escape the rough waters. And things might get worse before they get better.

But to "try everything" impatiently, without allowing enough time for results to come to fruition, is a lot like a ship captain lost at sea without a compass, going around in circles endlessly, finally concluding that dry land must be a myth because he just can't find it.

ok it was a short thread, i read it, NUTS???????? are you serious, Jack? i can eat almonds and lots of them and fight candida? for the love of pete, finally something easy lol

Jack, i just want to say i'm sorry to you personally, it really was like being possessed, i wasn't myself, tho i have always had the ability to tear into someone, it's just that i don't use it, as i am generally kind hearted

i think being out here in the country and whatever else was involved in the 'demon' leaving lol means that i have to start a long range plan to get myself into a tiny house off the grid, out away from cell phone towers and power lines... i always knew that the city made me crazy, but i didn't know why, and now, being out here, even with the smart meters and the cell phone tower, it's still not as bad as the city, and i have to make some plans to move for my husband's retirement, research commencing in 3.... 2.... 1....

and i just had to crawl onto the bed from the floor and wait for the wounds to heal, and God help anybody who talks to me during those times

if Jack had a file section i would post an astrological chart a friend did for me, not that i believed in it, but it was so right on it was scary... even where she made a mistake, it stuck out like a sort thumb as not being me...

i'm a weird one, mr grinch

### nicld — 2014-11-09T00:19:21+0200

Check out what I am doing to get more electrons.
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/)

You may feel better just knowing that you are going to do something to change your situation even if it is a while down the road. I know that we will be moving in 4 years and even though my home situation is not that bad (I already live in the country) I know that it can be better.

Good luck.

### Rain — 2014-11-09T11:04:11+0200

so i was going to buy an audible book to listen to tonight, i didn't want another diet book, so i started looking to see if there was anything about protecting myself from EMF, there wasn't... so i said a prayer for God to show me a good book
and i bought this
[http://pamgrout.com/books-r-me-my-career-as-an-author/e-squared/](http://pamgrout.com/books-r-me-my-career-as-an-author/e-squared/)

the first one has been sent out and i started listening to the beginning of the second one
and it talked about these people that manifested HUGE cabbages, and i thought of my zucchini that just FLOORED me today, that i took as a sign from God lol i thought 'oh goodness, should i write to her and ask her if you can get the blessing even before you ask for it coz God knows you're going to ask for it later that day anyway? hahaha
so i had to add in to my request that i can get more than one gift
kinda freaky tho
and on Friday as we pulled up the road toward our RV i saw a full moon like i have never seen in my whole life, i mean it was unreal, so huge it actually scared me a bit!

so yeah, i have 2 days to wait for the first experiment, and then 2 days for each other one if the pattern holds true
i will keep you posted

> Elaine, I know where you are and how you feel. I was there as well. What worked for me was to stop researching and torturing myself and instead spend my time in the nature, sleep outdoors, read good books and spend time with people. During that time I as well learned how to instruct my own bpdy to do do what I wanted. Visualization. It is just as simple as it sounds you think and imagine about what would you like your body to do and the body will eventually do it. The power of thoughts, exactly is JK said, good thoughts equals to good health. There is a lot you can do and discover in this sense. it helped me tremendously to calm down and regain the faith in myself. I remember I was affarid to drive my kids in the car because I was not sure whether my legs and my head will work good enoung to be able to manage the car. Absolutely surreal for someone as tpugh and strong as I was for my whole previous life. To trust to myself and to trust that the enate programms encoded in my cellls will deal with my complex issues much better than any supplement or doctor brought me peace of mind and lifted from my life fear from future and reaper. This finally allowed me to start enjoying life independently of the conditions or situations. Send yourself(cells) love and the love comes to you.

Rain/Elaine, I am so sorry for your struggles. I don't have anything to add - you got some amazing input from a lot of Rockstars on this site and it is amazing how kind people are to take their precious time to support you.

I do hope you might consider even one thing you can do for your health. I love Kovita's suggestions here. No one can control your thoughts...you choose them.

Good luck, and please don't give up!

when i was a young girl, maybe 16, i looked up at the stars on a beautiful night out at the lake, and had a realization that the whole world was a trick, and not a good one.

i have always lived in fear of this world

when i was listening to another audio book by florence scovel shinn she was telling a story of helping a girl... a girl who's landlord wouldn't turn on the heat and her arthritic mother was freezing. So FSS said 'God is your landlord' and the heat came on later that day without her having to ask the landlord

just a short story, but a lightbulb went on in my head, a big HUGE lightbulb, and i said 'God is my world'... yes, from that point forward, the world fell away and was replaced with God, and I knew, somehow I was living in a different place... or that the light had come on in the dark and now i could see. it was a revelation that i am still processing, but i'm safe in my world now because it's God.... the one i was in before... well that was the devil maybe

# Thread: How we transform energy explained for dummies! (Like me)

### Konish — 2014-10-19T03:40:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-we-transform-energy-explained-for-dummies-like-me.11936/post-144235>

"If you can't explain it simply, you don't understand it well enough." Albert Einstein

Can the intelligent gentlemen and women comment on my primate understanding of how we transform energy. Ive written all the forms i can think of! I know i have things wrong below, have been reading for a long time the quantum details, but i feel now a birdeye view of all the processes are needed before one can go deeper!

Protonicity:

1\. Sunlight enters our skin and underneath it is water. Water is charged separated, creating an EZ zone. Collagen acts as a hydrophilic material, our P semiconductor
2\. As water is split, protonicty can flow throughout the whole body, within the collagen carbon nanotubes, via Grotthuss mechanism. The carbon nanotubes already penetrate all cell wall, so effectively this connects to every single cell and inch of body
3\. The main purpose of protonicity is mitophagy, telomere action and blood flow. Not for actual energy production. More so a signal of external environmental.

Energy production for daily life:

1\. Food we eat makes ATP. ATP simply unfolds protein so water can bind to -NH and -COO moieties. The purpose is so water can ionize via antiparallel fashion. The ionized form of water allows energy transfer.
2\. The energy transfer specifically cans from water's excited state. This difference between ground and excited state is what the mitochondria use for redox reactions. The keys to excited states are from IR light, hydrogen bonding angles and correct magnetic field.
3\. DHA is a requirement to make use of converting sunlight into electric current
4\. Vortices in 3rd ventricle of the brain also structure water internally changing energy states. So that's why people who drink terrible water/never go in the sun/never ground can still be alive as we can structure it internally!

Grounding
1\. When our barefeet touches the grass or sand the electrons transfer into our body. The actual site of transfer is through the moisture expelled from our pores, this completes a conductive circuit. The electrons flows through the sweat, eventually through basement membrane into blood stream. Once in the blood it raises zeta potential of blood and is transported around the body to fulfil any neutralization/reaction.
2\. There is also the aspect of the protective field we plug into from approx 350 volts between the Earth and the top of our head

Oxygen
1\. As its paramagnetic it also provides a source of energy

Piezoelectric Current
1\. From the Schumann resonance, gravity, movement and also sunlight. Another form of converting energy from environment.

Magnetic field:
This grand scheme can is overrun by the magnetic field we are surrounded by, as Maxwell’s laws state, if we live in a in-congruent magnetic field, we may have a lot of current but it doesn't go to the right places and all cell signalling is messed up. Overriding all of above. The King of all.

# Thread: Why are there 13 so called Essential Vitamins?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-there-13-so-called-essential-vitamins.11937/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2014-10-19T08:10:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-there-13-so-called-essential-vitamins.11937/post-144241>

What fundamentally are Vitamins? Have you ever pondered this question? This [Wiki entry](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vitamin) gives the conventional view..

> Vitamins have diverse biochemical functions. Some, such as vitamin D, have hormone-like functions as regulators of mineral metabolism, or regulators of cell and tissue growth and differentiation (such as some forms of vitamin A). Others function as [antioxidants](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antioxidants) (e.g., [vitamin E](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vitamin_E) and sometimes [vitamin C](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vitamin_C)).[\[4\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vitamin#cite_note-Bender-4) The largest number of vitamins, the [B complex](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/B_complex) vitamins, function as precursors for enzyme [cofactors](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cofactor_\(biochemistry\)), that help enzymes in their work as [catalysts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Catalyst) in [metabolism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metabolism). In this role, vitamins may be tightly bound to [enzymes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Enzyme) as part of [prosthetic groups](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prosthetic_group): For example, [biotin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biotin) is part of enzymes involved in making [fatty acids](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fatty_acid). They may also be less tightly bound to enzyme catalysts as [coenzymes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coenzyme), detachable molecules that function to carry [chemical groups](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Functional_group) or electrons between molecules. For example, [folic acid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folic_acid) may carry [methyl](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Methyl_group), [formyl](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Formyl), and [methylene](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Methylene_group) groups in the cell. Although these roles in assisting enzyme-substrate reactions are vitamins' best-known function, the other vitamin functions are equally important.[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vitamin#cite_note-5)

and this [table](http://www.chemistry.patent-invent.com/chemistry/vitamins.html) gives the official chemical name, formula, solubility, molar mass, melting point, boiling point and density.

So, why at this point of evolution are there this particular number? Are there many more to be discovered?

Another riddle for all the so-called essential vitamins (essential nutrients that we lack the ability to synthesize). **What is the evolutionary advantage to this lack?** **When in evolutionary time did we lose this ability for each of these vitamins and Why?** For vitamin C, we have very limited compensating mechanisms, such as extra absorptive ability, or storage capacity. Whereas for vitamin B12, we have nearly a dozen extra proteins in our physiology whose unique purposes seem to be to absorb, transport, store, and use vitamin B12. This suggests that B12 might have been a vitamin for a very very long time, especially compared to an upstart like vitamin C.

[Vitamin-K2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vitamin_K2) for example has many important roles and in this [article](http://file:///C:/Users/PaulG/Downloads/0c960531f80dc26688000000.pdf) is identified as an important mitochondrial electron carrier in fruit flies but how well do we understand Vitamins from a Quantum Evolutionary Scale?

> Human UBIAD1 localizes to mitochondria and converts vitamin K1 to vitamin K2. Vitamin K2 is best known as a cofactor in blood coagulation, but in bacteria it is a membrane-bound electron carrier. Whether vitamin K2 exerts a similar carrier function in eukaryotic cells is unknown. We identified_Drosophila_ UBIAD1/Heix as a modifier of _pink1_, a gene mutated in Parkinson’s disease that affects mitochondrial function. We found that vitamin K2 was necessary and sufficient to transfer electrons in_Drosophila_ mitochondria. _Heix_ mutants showed severe mitochondrial defects that were rescued by vitamin K2, and, similar to ubiquinone, vitamin K2 transferred electrons in _Drosophila_ mitochondria, resulting in more efficient adenosine triphosphate (ATP) production. Thus, mitochondrial dysfunction was rescued by vitamin K2 that serves as a mitochondrial electron carrier, helping to maintain normal ATP production

The article [**A quantum theory for the irreplaceable role of docosahexaenoic acid in neural cell signalling throughout evolution**](http://www.ars.usda.gov/SP2UserFiles/person/4986/set4/Final%20DHA%202012.pdf) by Crawford et al attempts a Quantum explanation for DHA's extreme conservation over 600 million years

> **abstract**
> Six hundred million years ago, the fossil record displays the sudden appearance of intracellular detail
> and the 32 phyla. The ‘‘Cambrian Explosion’’ marks the onset of dominant aerobic life. Fossil
> intracellular structures are so similar to extant organisms that they were likely made with similar
> membrane lipids and proteins, which together provided for organisation and specialisation. While
> amino acids could be synthesised over 4 billion years ago, only oxidative metabolism allows for the
> synthesis of highly unsaturated fatty acids, thus producing novel lipid molecular species for specialised
> cell membranes.
> Docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) provided the core for the development of the photoreceptor, and
> conversion of photons into electricity stimulated the evolution of the nervous system and brain. Since
> then, DHA has been conserved as the principle acyl component of photoreceptor synaptic and neuronal
> signalling membranes in the cephalopods, fish, amphibian, reptiles, birds, mammals and humans. This
> extreme conservation in electrical signalling membranes despite great genomic change suggests it was
> DHA dictating to DNA rather than the generally accepted other way around.
> We offer a theoretical explanation based on the quantum mechanical properties of DHA for such
> extreme conservation. The unique molecular structure of DHA allows for quantum transfer and
> communication of p-electrons, which explains the precise depolarisation of retinal membranes and
> the cohesive, organised neural signalling which characterises higher intelligence

and Dr. Kruse talked about the importance of electron tunneling and DHA back in 2012
[http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-5-paradigm-drifts-paradigm-shifts-epi-paleo/](http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-5-paradigm-drifts-paradigm-shifts-epi-paleo/)

can similar arguments be made for Vitamins? Dr. Kruse has explained how nature harnessed the sun to generate Vitamin D in [http://jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-7-vitamin-d/](http://jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-7-vitamin-d/) by utilising UV frequencies of the sun. But maybe it is not all about light..this article on [evolutionary insights on B12](http://www.damtp.cam.ac.uk/user/gold/pdfs/b12.pdf) from a genetics perspective says..

> **Abstract**
> Vitamin B12 (cobalamin) is a dietary requirement for humans because it is an essential cofactor for two enzymes, methylmalonyl-CoA mutase and methionine synthase (METH). Land plants and fungi neither synthesize or require cobalamin because they do not contain methylmalonyl-CoA mutase, and have an alternative B12-independent methionine synthase (METE). Within the algal kingdom, approximately half of all microalgal species need the vitamin as a growth supplement, but there is no phylogenetic relationship between these species, suggesting that the auxotrophy arose multiple times through evolution. We set out to determine the underlying cellular mechanisms for this observation by investigating elements of B12 metabolism in the sequenced genomes of 15 different algal species, with representatives of the red, green, and brown algae, diatoms, and coccolithophores, including both macro- and microalgae, and from marine and freshwater environments. From this analysis, together with growth assays, we found a strong correlation between the absence of a functional METE gene and B12 auxotrophy. The presence of a METE unitary pseudogene in the B12-dependent green algae Volvox carteri and Gonium pectorale, relatives of the B12-independent Chlamydomonas reinhardtii, suggest that B12 dependence evolved recently in these lineages. In both C. reinhardtii and the diatom Phaeodactylum tricornutum, growth in the presence of cobalamin leads to repression of METE transcription, providing a mechanism for gene loss. Thus
> varying environmental conditions are likely to have been the reason for the multiple independent origins of B12 auxotrophy in these organisms. Because the ultimate source of cobalamin is from prokaryotes, the selective loss of METE in different algal lineages will have had important physiological and ecological consequences for these organisms in terms of their dependence on bacteria

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-19T16:08:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-there-13-so-called-essential-vitamins.11937/post-144278>

Vitamin D is a misnomer. It’s not a vitamin, first of all. Vitamins are supposed to be nitrogen containing compounds that act as co-enzymes which your get from your diet. We don’t make Vitamin D from food. Some foods have Vitamin D but it is rare. We make it from a specific wavelength of sunlight. Moreover, Vitamin D does not contain nitrogen. Vitamin A, C, and E don't either, but unlike Vitamin C and E, it is also not a co-factor in humans. It does however interact with DHA and Vitamin A via the RXR receptors in the brain.

Vitamin A acts like a co-factor by being a precursor of a co-factor in the eye photoreceptor enzymes. It also gets processed in a controlled manner into hormones for other parts of the body.

Vitamin D acts just like a hormone would, by raising blood calcium levels and helping cell differentiation across the body. But unlike Vitamin A's hormonal form, called the retinoic acids, every other hormone’s production is not produced on demand by homeostatic mechanisms in the body. Vitamin D is not. We need the sun to get it.

### PaulG — 2014-10-19T16:51:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-there-13-so-called-essential-vitamins.11937/post-144285>

I found this question at the end of one of the human biology text book chapters about evolution and thought it was quite interesting. I bet there's probably a lot of DHA in those fat pads!

> The Inuit people of the Arctic circle have small pads of fat around their eye sockets, which push the eyelids forward, so that they cover more of the eyeball than is the case in people who live in more temperate climates. It has been argued that these pads help to protect the eyes from the damaging brilliance of a
> snow-bound landscape.
>
> How would the evolution of the fat pads be explained by an evolutionary
> biologist? Include the following terms in your answer: natural selection; adaptive characteristic; survival advantage; fitness; evolution.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-19T22:39:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/50-grams-or-protein-no-can-do.11940/post-144311>

read what I wrote to Elaine then in her thread......a lot of it is operable to you.

If DHA isn’t present we lose the electric charge and the associated magnetic force associated with it in our mitochondria. People with this issue lose the ability to change sunlight frequencies signals into electric signals. Soon you will see why this a major deal in the next blog. So that they can change native EMF into electrical signals and back to light in other places within cells. When this situation occurs chronically, circadian signaling is further destroyed within the CNS and within cell membranes system wide and significant disease results.

The less ideal your mitochondria hold their electric charges, the higher the fat version your diet has to be. I sense this is your main issue and why protein is a problem. Your change two years ago might have helped you a bit so your not as bad off as others. As your environment changes and Ca2+ efflux slows, mitochondrial function improves, the lower the fat content needs to be and proteins can rise in your dietary template; as the environments improves further and less calcium is effluxed, your mitochondrial function improves further, the protein levels in your diet **_should naturally decrease_** because your ubiquination pathways improve so you don't not need to replace a lot of protein in your tissues. You might be here. I have no idea what your redox Rx is. You might want to look at your labs to get a feeling.

Modern research has pointed out that people who are big time exerciser's don't need a lot of protein. This is where a lot of paleo meat heads make a huge error. As you improve further, you begin to be able to exercise more, building your muscle mass as well, and then you can add "seasonal carbs" back to your template with out risk. This all assumes that you stop effluxing calcium…………..and today that situation is now very rare because humans love technology.

I wonder what's the case with someone like Inger, who obviously gets a lot of protein for her size, lots of fat too, and just lots of calories in general. Then there's also a question of the source of that protein. I don't understand how protein from fish differ from amino acids from beef. Conventional wisdom states that they are all the same, since they get broken down into amino acids anyway. But of course, they are present as large clusters of molecules in a complex tertiary or quartinery structure, so I'd bet there would be a difference due to the EMF altering compounds present in some protein sources and not others (eg: iodine in seafood).

In my own case, I can quite easily down 2kg of salmon, but if you ask me to do the same with 2kg of beef (lean or fatty), I'd struggle a little bit (though I still can). Hmmm .... DHA as a buffer for excess protein consumption? Am I getting a "protein overdose" with the beef but not with the salmon?

### yewwei.tan — 2014-10-21T02:29:38+0300

> [yewwei.tan said:](/goto/post?id=144333)

Is she in a state of protein excess, and is just capable of dealing with that excess, or does she really need that extra protein for tissue protein synthesis?
>
> Then there's also a question of the source of that protein. I don't understand how protein from fish differ from amino acids from beef. Conventional wisdom states that they are all the same, since they get broken down into amino acids anyway. But of course, they are present as large clusters of molecules in a complex tertiary or quartinery structure, so I'd bet there would be a difference due to the EMF altering compounds present in some protein sources and not others (eg: iodine in seafood).
>
> In my own case, I can quite easily down 2kg of salmon, but if you ask me to do the same with 2kg of beef (lean or fatty), I'd struggle a little bit (though I still can). Hmmm .... DHA as a buffer for excess protein consumption? Am I getting a "protein overdose" with the beef but not with the salmon?

Relevant comment from Jack on the QH FB group (full comment below with formatting edits) -- [https://www.facebook.com/groups/872...d=883110075035221&offset=0&total\_comments=103](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/881516561861239/?comment_id=883110075035221&offset=0&total_comments=103)

The worse your mitochondria are the higher the fat version of the keto diet has to be.

As your mitochondria improve the lower the fat content needs to be as the protein levels can increase.

As you improve further the protein levels in your diet decrease because your ubiquination pathways improve so you don't not need to replace a lot of protein.

As you improve you begin to be able to exercise more as well and then you can add seasonal carbs back to your template. **The more you exercise the less protein you actually need.** This is another thing that the paleo kids do not understand because their "leaders" have no idea how ubiquination rates are tied to the amount of exercise you do. Ask [Bill Lagakos](https://www.facebook.com/bill.lagakos) if this is bad advice. I do not want anyone buying what I am selling. I want you to all know why I say what I do. My understanding is based upon a different scale of science.
​

Ubiquitin is basically a protein that attaches itself to another protein, selectively marking it for degradation. ie: if you want less protein turnover, you want the ubiquitin-proteasome pathway to be less active.

The paper '**Response of the ubiquitin-proteasome pathway to changes in muscle activity**' is a great read for the basics of Ubiquination and how skeletal muscle activity affects these rates -- [http://ajpregu.physiology.org/content/288/6/R1423](http://ajpregu.physiology.org/content/288/6/R1423)

Most of the time, exercise of all types is shown to increase ubiquination. We do find however that repeated exercise leads to adaptations that reduce ubiquination. This may be why you need to be well enough to exercise, since the introduction to exercise will impose a net negative stressor on the body as it clears out bad proteins that have accumulated. After that, the remaining proteins stay strong so long as they can continue to retain a high redox potential.

**Interesting quote from paper**: Viral proteins are known to mediate specific substrate-E2/E3 interactions, thereby promoting ubiquitination
Heh, so viruses can make protein turnover more likely to enable proliferation of their own RNA.

The only fix that I can see is to ensure that your proteins retain a high redox potential, and therefore not get marked for replacement by ubiquitin. I think DHA accomplishes this pretty well, see below ...

\---

I found this paper '**DHA Inhibits Protein Degradation More Efficiently than EPA by Regulating the PPAR-gamma/NF-kappa-beta Pathway in C2C12 Myotubes**' -- [http://www.hindawi.com/journals/bmri/2013/318981/](http://www.hindawi.com/journals/bmri/2013/318981/)

Granted, it's done with mouse contractile protein model, which should mimic human skeletal tissue to a large enough degree. They basically demonstrated that both EPA and DHA was able to limit NFkb expression by up-regulating PPAR-g expression. They proved that it is specifically PPAR-g up-regulation that is the key by using a PPAR-g knockout model.

The result is that at concentrations of about 400 uM of DHA in the tissue, muscle protein degradation was reduced by 49%. 500 uM of EPA reduced protein degradation by 30%. Those are very significant numbers

More EPA or DHA than the values given showed a negative effect, suggesting an optimal dose for each -- Figure from paper: [http://www.hindawi.com/journals/bmri/2013/318981/fig1/](http://www.hindawi.com/journals/bmri/2013/318981/fig1/) (**Caveat**: human tissue will likely have a different optimal dose. My personal opinion is that given DHA abundance, the body will automatically regulate that does in skeletal tissue)

Note that even in the PPAR-g knockout model, DHA and EPA were still able to reduce degradation rates by up to 17%. ie: if you're sick, and have poor PPAR-g expression, you still benefit from eating DHA.

\---

PPAR-gamma is the sole focus of Levee 12 of 'The QUILT' -- [http://jackkruse.com/the-quilthow-to-beat-agin/](http://jackkruse.com/the-quilthow-to-beat-agin/) . It was discussed in good detail in 'How to find your Inner Masterpiece' blog -- [http://jackkruse.com/how-to-find-your-inner-masterpiece/](http://jackkruse.com/how-to-find-your-inner-masterpiece/)

sidenote: PPAR-delta seems to be important for controlling skeletal tissue metabolism. I'll probably have to eventually read this (long) paper on the PPARs to get more details. -- [http://pharmrev.aspetjournals.org/content/61/3/373.full](http://pharmrev.aspetjournals.org/content/61/3/373.full)​

NFkb has been mentioned many many times on the blog. I think the 2 most related blogs are:

-   EE 12 -- [http://jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-12-battery-charged/](http://jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-12-battery-charged/)
-   OSF 5 - [http://jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-5-prions-are-compliant-design-flaws/](http://jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-5-prions-are-compliant-design-flaws/)

Anyway, NFkb is needed for oxidative signalling, but as usual, over-expression leads to lots of expensive cell turnover.

\=====
**Back to my original questions
**
Now I think the answer is: Yes, DHA helps directing reduce the harm that any excess protein can cause.

I still don't know whether amino acids derived from beef are the same as those derived from salmon.

Random Tensegrity 6 excerpts:

When iodine meets water that has been charged separated by IR light or by the hydrophilic proteins within the dura matter a massive amount of H+ is made in the CSF of the brain.​

A cell uses hydrogen and oxygen to un-condense our protein polymers, ever so slightly, to allow life to exist.​

It turns out all proteins are hydrated in life.

Water also polarizes around proteins at all times and that action reduces the hydrogen bond length in that intracellular water to form coherent domains
​

Seems like the job of these protein polymers really is just to be as hydrophilic as possible to allow for charge separation.

Now, the proteins in a piece of salmon belly should obey all these principles as well. The greater cell membrane fluidity of the salmon over the beef (due to the lower O3:O6 ratio) should allow for better assimilation of nutrients like DHA into our own cells. But does eating more hydrophilic proteins directly led to our own proteins becoming more hydrophilic?

\----

Too many posts today already. Need to eat DHA and revisit this thread tomorrow.

### yewwei.tan — 2014-10-29T08:39:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/50-grams-or-protein-no-can-do.11940/post-145524>

> [ssj3 said:](/goto/post?id=144517)
>
> For the narrow single focus of consuming 50g+ of protein for circadian ryhthm... could you "hack" this by consuming leucine?

My guess is that the answer is "not as effectively".

The 'How does the Leptin Reset Work' article talks about some details -- [http://jackkruse.com/how-does-the-leptin-rx-work/](http://jackkruse.com/how-does-the-leptin-rx-work/) .

Dr Kruse also briefly mentioned leucine in the 'King of the King' post -- [http://jackkruse.com/central-leptin-dominance-part-3-king-of-hill/](http://jackkruse.com/central-leptin-dominance-part-3-king-of-hill/)

My take is that when you want to correct a circadian mismatch, you should use a strategy that hits as many circadian entrainment systems as possible. (If you're not mismatched, I personally don't there is much left to optimise).

Eating a bolus of real-food protein gets a natural mix of proteins, and stimulates the vagus nerve. Dr Kruse talked about the "digital" and "analogue" circadian systems in the EE4 blog post, and how the vagus nerve linked the two -- [http://jackkruse.com/energy-and-epigenetics-4-light-water-magnetism/](http://jackkruse.com/energy-and-epigenetics-4-light-water-magnetism/) . I don't think this effect is significant when using pure leucine, which is usually in powder form (dissolved in liquid)

Then there's this off-hand comment about how leucine deficiency mimics caloric restriction -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/leucine-deficiency.5602/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/leucine-deficiency.5602/)

Leucine is also highly insulinogenic. I don't know what implications that has to correcting circadian rhythms.

This makes me think back to my post about "Tensegrity #8 and insulin sensitivity" -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...sitivity-molybdenum-ubiquitin-pathways.12098/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/tensegrity-8-insulin-sensitivity-molybdenum-ubiquitin-pathways.12098/)

Does insulin actually reduce the charge potential on the cell membrane? Does a strong cell membrane charge "push away" glucose molecules?

Conventional knowledge says that insulin drives glucose into a cell when it binds to the insulin receptor. There are lots of theories behind insulin receptor activity, which I do not understand well at all.

I also don't understand why Leucine would raise both insulin and up-regulate the mTOR pathway at the same time -- [http://jn.nutrition.org/content/131/3/861S.long](http://jn.nutrition.org/content/131/3/861S.long)

Then I don't know why use zinc in insulin? How does this particular metal tie to cell membrane permeability?

The activation of insulin seems to be tied to the appropriate formation of 3 disulfide bonds to form the active form of insulin. This made me think of glutathione, which requires Zinc as a co-factor for its reducing power, and forms a disulfide bond when it itself is oxidised. Obviously there's a link between zinc and disulfide bond formation which I do not understand .... (and the corollarry is that I do not understand the periodic table arrangement)

# Thread: Struggling to digest raw fish
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/struggling-to-digest-raw-fish.11943/>
