Looks as though gravity took hold because it could be observed and measured in the 1700's and now we can observe and measure other things "below the surface" so to speak....Amazing how long science gets stuck on a model and or level.....

### yewwei.tan — 2014-10-27T09:58:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-think-gravity-is-emergent-here-is-a-clue.11995/post-145269>

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=145215)
>
> Looks as though gravity took hold because it could be observed and measured in the 1700's and now we can observe and measure other things "below the surface" so to speak....Amazing how long science gets stuck on a model and or level.....

Electric Gravity

Paraphrased excerpts/notes (the video is a critique of an article about worm holes between 2 black holes):

All the electric dipoles in protons and neutrons line up to produce Gravity

The direct electric force between particles occurs INSTANTLY .... "entanglement" is just an electric resonance between 2 subatomic particles, each compromised of an identical system of orbiting charged subparticles (an atom within an atom) regardless of their relative movement or distance apart.

This model, which separates the EM waves in the medium from the direct longitudinal connection between all matter in the universe (electrical connection) opens up new insights for physicists (_TYW: understatement of the last 2 centuries_ )

Black holes are a mathematical mistake equivalent to trying to divide by zero. Using gravity, which is a billion billion billion times weaker than the EM force (close to zero), to explain the concentrated forces in the middle of galaxies, and calling them black holes, is bound to give you answers that make no physical sense.

The wormholes are a geometric construct in more than 3 dimensions, so they have nothing to do with the real space that physicists and engineers have to deal with.

The laws of gravity are not fundamental, but arise from quantum entanglement.

Modern physics is in the business of inventing new particles and concepts in the hopes of winning a nobel prize.

Mathematics use dimensions to represent degrees of freedom. Time has no direction (it is not a dimensino). Joining space and time has no significance. So you cannot produce physical wormholes in space.
​

I REALLY need to start going through all their videos

### yewwei.tan — 2014-10-29T22:38:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-think-gravity-is-emergent-here-is-a-clue.11995/post-145569>

> [NeilBB said:](/goto/post?id=145552)
>
> Nice video. Thornhill is an interesting guy and has significant disagreements with Einstein, BTW. As did Tesla, whose quote:
>
> Jack has been paraphrasing a lot lately. When this discussion is all said and done, I'm not sure that even Einstein's theories will be any more safe from scrutiny and revision than everyone else...

Ha, I still need to process some of that stuff.

I was biased right from the start of the talk, when he invoked complexity theory to say that complex phenomena occurs as a result of simple rules. So much of the software I see is like that, eg: the TCP protocol that we're using to post to this forum has some remarkably simple algorithms controlling it's parameters. I was reading about the rollout of CoDel last year (used to manage bufferbloat), and I could understand it pretty easily.

When I could never do is discover an algorithm like that; Simple things are very hard to discover.

-   Firstly, because we seem to have a bias against simple things -- there's an arrogance that something that can be explained to a high school student must be "too unsophisticated to describe reality".

    Of course, this is my mind is a failure to understand that it is almost impossible to control complex phenomena (as nature does) if the underlying rules are complex.

-   Secondly, out of a huge set of possibilities, you have to pick out the few that actually matter. Because of emergent behaviour (cannot work backwards from a result), there is no way to figure out which factors are significant by examining their products. We're left with trial and error based on educated guesses

Despite any disagreements that Thornhill may have with Einstein, E=mc2 and the photoelectric effect are simple concepts that will likely stand the test of time. Any modifications will be additive, and we won't have to throw away E=mc2 like the way we'll have to throw away the notion of a black hole.

Because of emergent behaviour (cannot work backwards from a result), there is no way to figure out which factors are significant by examining their products. We're left with trial and error based on educated guesses
>

Any modifications will be additive, and we won't have to throw away E=mc2 like the way we'll have to throw away the notion of a black hole.

[http://www.holoscience.com/wp/synopsis/synopsis-11-some-basics/](http://www.holoscience.com/wp/synopsis/synopsis-11-some-basics/)

Thornhill's view:

> **E = mc2**
> Einstein’s famous mathematical expression E=mc2, equating energy and mass is known by almost everyone. However, most textbooks go on to use the word “matter” in place of “mass.” But nowhere has it been shown that mass and matter are interchangeable. In fact, we are entirely ignorant of what constitutes the mass of an object. So it is inadmissible to imply that energy and matter are interchangeable. The ultimate expression of this idea led to the nonsense of the big bang. It seems simpler and more sensible to suggest that both nuclear and chemical energy is released or absorbed by the rearrangement of the resonant orbits of charged particles. It is then common sense to suggest that mass is the measured response of a system of charged particles to an external electrostatic force. The more massive an object, the more the electrostatic force contributes to the elastic deformation of its protons, neutrons and electrons, rather than their acceleration. This is the phenomenon seen in particle accelerators and conventionally attributed to relativistic effects. But relativity reduces to classical physics in a universe where the electrostatic force has near-infinite speed. The first question to be asked is – if it is that simple, why hasn’t it been thought of long ago? The answer seems to lie in the propensity for mathematical theory to supersede common sense and observation. There is also a problem of language when mathematicians attempt to provide real meaning for their symbols.

Einstein's relativity equations have held up experimentally, as has the quantum theory! But the interpretation of those equations and their application to reality is a different matter. [Look at the collapse of the Copenhagen interpretation of quantum mechanics now underway.](http://www.techtimes.com/articles/15553/20140914/quantum-mechanics-orthodoxy-of-copenhagen-interpretation-may-be-replaced-by-pilot-wave-theory.htm) I think the implications of General Relativity will also be in trouble soon. I think Jack would agree with that. Special Relativity may be a different story. Its equations will hold up for sure, but will their meaning as they are now understood in terms of spacetime? I don't know...

### NeilBB — 2014-10-30T01:17:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-think-gravity-is-emergent-here-is-a-clue.11995/post-145599>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=145594)
>
> any measurement of anything perceivable and measurable only serve to reduce thw whole into incremental pieces to understand it fragmentally, which appears to be the default state that the modern human brain is tuned to in order to rationalise. all science (whilst we need it in the current world state for medicine etc) only serves to take the immeasurable whole, measure it as fragments, and attempt to explain the whole again using the sum of its parts.

That is undeniably factually true. That is the method of human cognition.

But I would maintain that the Lipton/Bohm implication that this makes the knowledge gained thusly somehow less valid or invalid because of that is a modern philosophical myth. Such an attitude would denigrate any epistemological method at all and stems from a false philosophical standard of what constitutes knowledge...

The whole reason we need epistemology is to validate human conceptual knowledge gained in this incremental, disintegrated way, and to reintegrate it properly into a cohesive whole, i.e., keep it tied to reality. It is very easy for it to become disconnected at any point, as I tried to explain in that blog post...

Philosophical epistemology collapsed in the 19th century (after and because of German revolutionary philosopher Kant). Thats a whole'nother story, but suffice it to say that the resulting epistemological corruption of most of the quantum physicists, most notably Neils Bohr, is in fact what lead them to all get lost in mathematics and lose sight of WTF they were actually talking about. Hence, we got the bullshit story from Copenhagen that is just now beginning to be unraveled...

### yewwei.tan — 2014-10-30T04:37:38+0200

The video I linked earlier also talks briefly about space-time -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...is-emergent-here-is-a-clue.11995/#post-145269](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/why-do-i-think-gravity-is-emergent-here-is-a-clue.11995/#post-145269)

Specifically, this excerpt which I took:

Mathematics use dimensions to represent degrees of freedom. Time has no direction (it is not a dimension). Joining space and time has no significance. So you cannot produce physical wormholes in space.
​

Then, Ron Hatch tackled Relativity, length contraction, time dilation, etc ... during the Electric Universe 2013 conference.

Very math heavy and I don't understand anything , but I'm sure some of the answers I seek are in there.

I don't know why, but that description feels at ease with the idea that "time can move in both directions". It's almost like saying that absent a stimulus, a particle simply "waits" until it requires something to be done, and only then does it react. The notion of condensed matter somehow gives me the idea of a higher proportion of particles "waiting around not doing anything". Stronger magnetism or gravity (electrostatic potential) would "warp time" but allowing for this condensation of matter to occur. (And of course, I don't know what I'm talking about )

Then the 'Electricity of LIfe' Panel raised even more points:

Does the time directionality variance in quantum mechniam scale from small particles electrons to larger systems like the heart or the brain? Rupert Shelldrake seems to think so, but we definitely don't have the language to describe it exactly yet.

### Dewey C. Layman Jr. — 2019-08-07T09:24:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-think-gravity-is-emergent-here-is-a-clue.11995/post-275561>

This thread should be revisited.

Somebody mentioned special relativity being clearly correct. It actually is not. Neither is general relativity. This has been observed and shown to be false with newtons bucket experiment and the fact that gravity has 0 affect of light waves. Yes, gravitational lensing is false. In the end, Einstein actually did not feel that his theories were very sound. People dont seem to talk about that much. The truth is Einstein had integrity in this way but his work is very unscientific and very false. Getting rid of our standards of measurement and making everything relative is not physics. There are no black holes, neutron stars, gravitons, antimatter particles, gluons, etc. There are 3 fundamental particles: electrons, protons, and neutrinos.

Photons are a concept and are physically possible. A particle cannot have no mass and travel through a vaccum. It needs a medium by which to perturb. Light merely acting as if it has a duality does not mean that it is both a wave and particle. It is neither. Light is not understood to this day.

Our universe is likely smaller than we think and is static NOT expanding based on an electric model of the universe. Gravity emerges from the electric force. The electric dipoles that we have between our feet and the ground is where this force emerges. It is what keeps us on the ground. It acts the same way that magnetism does and YES that means gravity is not only attractive but is repulsive but it IS much much weaker than magnetism. This should give hint as to why planets and other planetary bodies will rarely, if ever, collide because like charges on the surfaces of bodies will repel. Bodies that get too close release electric discharges that change the mass and gravity of these bodies. Hint: Chicxulub Crater was not an impact crater. It was an electric discharge. Did it happen millions of years ago? Also no. More like in the thousands.

Do we know the age of the universe or our own planet? The age we think it is needs to be questioned big time because ill bet its way off the mark. So whats the human story? Whats the mitochondria story? It isnt known yet. We do have great reason to believe that our current sun isnt our first one and isnt the one we are actually optimized to. Saturn, our brown dwarf star, is the star that has been mentioned in ancient civilization. Ra is not the current sun god. It is synonymous with Kronos, or Saturn. Our world along with mars, venus (spoiler: a comet created by saturn), and jupiter were a nice happy family living in a giant red anode glow created by Saturn. Our skies were purple. Crops, fruit, vegetation was plentiful because of red and UVs affect on photosynthesis. During this time we actually saw saturn mars and venus very close to our planet. Thats how ancient civilization knew there was giant electrical scarring on Mars that they called Scarface. How else could they have known this scar was there without telescopes?

This was known as the Golden Age when life flourished. A life lived in the red and purple. Want to know where most of the water on our planet came from? Answer: Saturn

Thats why Saturns rings contain water very similar to our own. Its also why Titan has a similar atmosphere now compared to Earth's atmosphere in the past.

Could this be why water in us is optimized to red light to make it refract at 270nm. Could this is be why our aromatic amino acids respond to UVC light? I think so. Does it have anything to do with deuterium in our blood? I dont know. That needs to be tested. UVC light doesnt come from our current sun. It probably came from our old one. Maybe now we can see a hint of this by looking at the absorption of these proteins.

We need to go back to classical physics. Chuck Einstein out and even what we think about quantum weirdness. It isnt weird at all. Its electric. There is only one fundamental force. The Electric Force.

I say this respectfully, April 2016 needs to be redone as well. Time to get rid of false beliefs.

### Dewey C. Layman Jr. — 2019-08-07T09:31:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-think-gravity-is-emergent-here-is-a-clue.11995/post-275562>

Gravitational Lensing False

I can sleep as long as I want to in a pitch black room.
I eat well, am active, 10 lbs overweight,but healthy.

Should I always go to bed and get up at the same time?

Please put up a picture - JK's rules .....no pic - no Jack!

Also start a journal in the journal section .....Dr. K. needs context ... and we do too if we are to offer some help.

your age and health history
your family health history
where do you live?
what is your non native EMF situation?
Fake blue lite?
exercise? if so - what?
sleep? how is it?
grounding? do you get outside in the am? do you hug tress???
what do you eat? drink? R/O water?
libido?

the best way to get the most out of jackkruse.com is to become a gold member \[if you can manage it\] .... you will then have access to all Jack's webinars 24/7 and a monthly live Q&A

Do you have Dr. K's book? The Epi-Paleo RX - it is a great way to get on board ...and gives you the first two years of the blog

Have you been reading Jack's blogs?

# Thread: Body voltage reading
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 91
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2014-10-28T03:56:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145383>

I am having some issues with figuring out my readings. For some reason I get a negative reading on my meter. I have the black and red hooked up right.

Anyone else get negative readings when grounding? Just want to make sure that I am doing this right since I am having doubts.

### Da-mo — 2014-10-28T07:20:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145393>

What happens when you swap the terminals over?

### nicld — 2014-10-28T15:43:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145422>

^^^^I did that last night but cannot remember what happened, I will try again tonight. I did double check the manual and with the hubby to make sure that I had the terminals right and they were.

Here is what I have been finding. I bought a copper grounding rod and drove it into the ground in my basement by another old pipe that was used when we had a shallow point well. Initially testing it and thought it was not too bad. Then I started testing upstairs. I was getting readings of about 400 mV in the living room (where all of the electronics are), not horrible but I still think too high. Odd thing was when I was sitting on the sofa and put my feet on the ottoman the reading dropped, very weird. It was lower in my kitchen and of course in the bedroom where the Magnetico is.

So this weekend I did some deeper diving into it. I shut off all the power to the house and retested. I got the exact same readings and this confused the hell out of me. I measure stuff for a living and this was stumping me. So I went back to the source, back to the grounding rod in the basement. With all the power off I touched the grounding terminal to the rod and it measured 500ish mV, WTF. Obviously it was NOT grounded. So I stuck the ground terminal in the ground right next to the rod and the readings went to 0. Back to square 1. I did have an old copper water pipe so I took that and stuck it outside in my garden beside the house and got 0 mV to negative mV readings. Ran a new wire from that pipe into the house and that is where I started to get more negative readings.

Last night I started testing the two wires right next to each other. The one that was attached to the pipe outside and one that was attached to the rod in the basement. I was getting negative readings from the one outside and 400’s again from the one it the basement. Based on some readings that I did, I attached the grounding terminal to the wire attached to the pipe outside and the reading terminal to the wire that is attached to the basement and got a reading over 800 mV. Then I put the ground on the wire in to the basement and the red to the wire going outside and got a negative 800mV reading. So I am seriously confused.

I also decided to test the readings on the hubby when we crawled in bed. Without the grounding strap on we both we at about 0 mV to just below 0mV. I then attached the strap to him and it read -200 mV, the same as I got so I know it is not just me.

Tonight I will be getting a new grounding rod since the one that it is the basement is way too deep to pull out now.

Like I said, I am a Quality Engineer which means I measure and analyze data but this one is just stumping me. Is it ok to have negative readings? I am thinking of borrowing one of the calibrated volt meters from work tonight and see if I get different results. The one I am using is also from work but it is not calibrated but really has not been used since we bought it.

### Da-mo — 2014-10-29T04:32:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145505>

From a link someone posted elsewhere in the forum . . .

> **Human cells are designed to run at about -20 millivolts (or pH of 7.35). The body has the ability to heal itself of many ailments if it is supplied with sound nutrition and proper cellular energy.**

[http://curezone.com/forums/am.asp?i=1609973](http://curezone.com/forums/am.asp?i=1609973)

### BTA — 2014-10-29T05:23:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145511>

I said that along time ago, nobody picked up on the fact you can't heal until your voltage is past -50. If you're over -20 going toward 0 you're in serious trouble.

### Penny — 2014-10-29T05:33:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145515>

Uh, I can't get my meter to read any negatives anywhere... if I ground, zero is as low as it ever gets... ? I stick the ground into ground and touch the red terminal - even stuck it in different kinds of water this morning - microwaved read something over 300 - R/O was around 3 as was alkaline... tap water was around 6...

I even piked an apple and I think it read 3... was looking for some way to get the bloody thing to measure negatively... but no go... ?

Maybe I should be measuring PH instead...

### BTA — 2014-10-29T05:34:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145516>

too funny - they are a bit tricky to operate. If you have a multimeter? look for mV setting.

### Da-mo — 2014-10-29T07:49:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145522>

After watching these videos it seems best to measure voltages at certain points on the body

links located bottom of this page [http://tennantbiomodulator.ca/Canadian-Disbtributor/Healing-is-Voltage](http://tennantbiomodulator.ca/Canadian-Disbtributor/Healing-is-Voltage)

### nicld — 2014-10-29T15:05:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145538>

Last night I bought a new grounding rod and stuck it in the ground right outside of the bedroom. I tested just the rod outside and got a 0 reading. Then I added the wire that I was going to use for it outside and again got a 0. Ran the wire in the house and tested it right next to the window. The readings changed a bit but my king side Magnetico is very close. I then went back into the living room and checked that and the reading went back up to 400+ mV. WTF.

Just to make sure it is not me I had my husband repeat the test and he got the same readings. But when I have shoes on in the living room the levels are closer to 0. I had basically the same results when I had all of the power off in the house. This weekend I will be repeating the power off testing with the new wire that it outside. I will also recheck myself when I have the grounding strap on in various areas of the house again.

So, does anyone know if the carpeting could be causing the higher readings? We will be replacing the carpeting when the dog dies and I am not opposed to putting in hardwood floors with a wool rug on it as I have laminate floors in 2 of the bedrooms.

I do feel a better about doing grounding at night with a wrist strap on while sleeping on the Magnetico. The reading for me and my husband while on the Magnetico and grounding strap on are still just over -200 mV.

### Inger — 2014-10-29T16:02:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145539>

> [BTA said:](/goto/post?id=145511)
>
> I said that along time ago, nobody picked up on the fact you can't heal until your voltage is past -50. If you're over -20 going toward 0 you're in serious trouble.

I did, BPA
I do already knew it had to be on the minus side, but it was cool you mentioned it too

### Penny — 2014-10-29T21:15:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145560>

Oh thanks for that I got -90 or so when I stuck it into an outlet and put my fingers around the red lead... the alkaline water was measuring 60 mV when I stuck the ground into the ground and the red lead into the water... probably not so hot then... ?

But, at least I'm using the right setting now...

### Penny — 2014-10-29T21:23:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145563>

**I'm sure you know \*way\* more about this than I, but maybe you have this:

[http://www.dirtyelectricity.ca/dirty\_electricity\_four\_components.htm](http://www.dirtyelectricity.ca/dirty_electricity_four_components.htm)

Ground Currents and Plumbing Current**

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Every time an electron leaves a power plant to create electricity for our homes, schools and offices it must return to create more electricity – fundamental law of physics. Today, the earth’s surface has a higher conductivity then the utility’s overburdened neutral return wire, so now it's estimated that as much as 70 to 80% of returning current travels back along the earth’s surface in what’s known as the “skin-effect.”

Many homes today are now affected by plumbing current . The happens when some of the current that would normally return through the electrical service line is instead channeled into the grounding system, where it returns to the transformer by way of alternate paths, including water pipes, the public water main and neighboring residences.

Two laws of physics support the fact that returning electrical ground current takes the path of least resistance and will take any and all paths - including into your home. This EMF energy can be conducted into a home's electrical system through water and gas pipes, phones lines, and other conductive objects instead of back to the substation through the neutral return wire as originally designed.

These electrical contaminates can then intermingle with a building’s 60 Hertz (Hz) electricity, polluting it it with undesirable high frequeicies and in some cases higher voltages. We are capacitively coupled to this with this run-away electric energy by simply being in a contaminate building or in contact with common household fixtures like sinks, bathtubs and other conductive objects.

### nicld — 2014-10-29T21:26:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145564>

This is the meter that I am using and it is set to the volt side. I am probably going to see if I can borrow a better one from our maintenance department and compare the two. Your measurements are only as good as the tool that you are using.

### nicld — 2014-11-02T18:52:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145937>

Finally getting around to watching the videos Da-Mo it is it actually making alot of sense. It makes sense why i might have been feeling worse in the beginning when he made the thumb analogy. Damaged cells are dying and getting replaced.

Still trying to figure out if the voltage in the cells can be tested with a body voltage meter. To me, it seems it should be different.

I checked my new grounding rod outside again and either I have something going on outside or the rods are not truly grounding. The one read 550 mV again but my copper pipe that I have still reads 0. So yesterday I disconnected the my wire from my grounding rod and attached it to the copper pipe again and did sleep with it and felt much better. I will keep checking it but pretty soon the ground will freeze and I will be out of luck. I will recheck my basement again and if all else fails, go sit down there for a while every night.

### NeilBB — 2014-11-07T20:01:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-146459>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=146458)
>
> Yep. My black ground terminal is either touching a grounding device or is attached to the copper wire that is attached to the grounding device outside. I hold the red positive terminal in my fingers or I have touched the inside of my check.
>
> When I lay on my Magnetico it drops to zero but when I attach the wrist strap my body voltage reading drops to negative numbers.
>
> Inger has had the same experience on her pad too, right Inger?
>
> I am also talking about it just dropping to -.2 volts so not real dramatic just odd. Also the highest reading I have ever had in my house with any of the grounding rods (which are in question) is 0.5 V. I feel like I am doing it right but it is not making sense to me right now.

If you are using 2 different grounding rods, one for the black terminal and one for the wrist strap, then that small voltage likely reflects a slight difference in potential actually in the ground at those two different sites, whether it's positive or negative just depends on which of the two rods is higher. There are no absolute voltages. It's all relative, even ground voltages. Calling ground "zero" is arbitrary. Personally, I don't think it's a big deal.

Also realize that if this is the case, then that small potential you measure on your skin will likely go away once you stop the measurement. The voltage is actually created by the process of measurement and being in contact with two slightly different grounds. When you are just lying on the Magnetico, not measuring, you skin voltage likely returns to zero.

I still think the RF could be an issue on the Magnetico though, like we talked about before.

The question I always had was just because you happen to look at the voltmeter at one time and it reads negative voltage - mine reads around -195 but it fluctuates and I have a Gardner Bender - it will tell you nothing about any spikes that happen in the middel of the night when say your neighbor's jacuzzi turns on - unless you use an oscilliscope and log it (they have free software) over a period of time, it seems to me you could be experiencing postive voltage and not even know what harm you are doing... an engineer on youtube did that and he figured out in the summer he always felt like crap because he was going barefoot and the ground voltage was nailing him - it's like a $200.oo oscilliscope - amazing really...

### Jack Kruse — 2014-11-09T20:48:36+0200

Re: cancer and the use of metformin:Warburg and Crabtree effect directly link to a broken DC current. It is all physics. Electrons move in circuits within and outside the body. the voltages are small in biophysics which upsets the belief system of most........on the order of 0.01 volts, but it is measurable. Go buy a high quality volt ohm meter and check for your self. You will see massive difference all over tissues. For example you nise is more positive than your mouth which is more negative. This sets up a classic cathode anode experiment that is the basis if redox electrochemistry. You'll find the voltage varies with respiration and HR. Why? magnetic flux in air flows due diatomic paramagnetic oxygen and in the blood DHA and O2 do the same. This means there has to be an opposing flow of metal ions between these circuits to close the circuit. Metformin provides those ions..............that is why it works. The electromagnetic force is amazing when you get how she works. Becker did.

So.........why is hypoxia bad for a eukaryote and good for a prokaryote? hypoxia = A low redox potential = a low delta psi. It is a state where water chemistry is very altered. Water is lacking inside and around cells, and cells are dehydrated. The water that is left behind has very little oxygen tension in it. This is why they call it hypoxia. It has altered levels of protons and/or electrons in it too. This metabolic situation leads to karyotype contraction. This means the number, **_size and shape of chromosomes becomes altered_**. The exclusion zone of water is smaller than it should be in most disease and it has an altered ability to allow protons to flow through ATP[synthetase Fo in mitochondria](http://www.bioinitiative.org/report/wp-content/uploads/pdfs/sec16_2012_Plausible_Genetic_Metabolic_Mechanisms.pdf). This does not allow water to bind to proteins and cell membranes properly and it alters their ability to sense the magnetic fields in their environment. It directly affects the mitochondrial magnetic sense. Sunlight and a magnetic field's flux flows are capable of ionizing water in the entire universe, including inside your cells. This very basic fact is not yet a biologic law, however, it is a natural physical law found everywhere we look. Biology and medicine just have not changed their gaze from mutated genes yet.

I agree, Inger. I expressed to Jack a long time ago that I was concerned that most people did not have the scientific rigor or background to be able to control and interpret their self-experimentation meaningfully and safely. Although, I love the idea in principle, I remain quite concerned about the execution. The problem is, though, since academia has so thoroughly lost its way, and since everyone's quantum environment is so unique, I see no alternative than for people to take such experimentation into their own hands. In a perfect world, I think that would be the job of a personal quantum physician. To help patients design and objectively interpret their biohacks and and then to intervene in other ways when clinically necessary. That's kind-of what Jack seems to do now, but his time and resources are very limited...

### nicld — 2015-06-18T20:30:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-168787>

**_UPDATE!!!!!!!!!!_**

So my living room is CRAP!!!!!!! Something is going on and now I am basically afraid to spend any time in there. I got a new meter that reads volts way better and am actually testing the AC current and NOT the DC current (yes, I am a blonde). Been testing it a couple of times and last night it was up to 11V in one area!!!! Yes it is carpeting so I am sure that may have something to do with it but when I turn off the breaker to the house, it drops to almost nothing. I do not get the same readings in the kitchen and the further away from the living room I get the better the readings get. And to think, I would lay on the floor before bed.

I am not getting my trifeild to really show anything expect near power cords. I did test with the power cords unplugged and still got a higher reading. When I turned off the breaker to the living room it dropped a bunch but was still a bit high.

Also, I have the computer grounding plug that Michael Neuert suggested and when I had that plugged into my laptop, my readings went over 20 (WTF!!!!!!).

So, next step will be to buy a outlet tester and see if the outlets in the living room are even grounded. I also got stuff to make a buzz stick that I will be trying to pinpoint where the high readings maybe coming from. [http://emfhelpcenter.com/](http://emfhelpcenter.com/)

I have already contacted 2 electricians to see what they know about EMF’s and if they can come out and give me an estimate on what is going on.

Then there is the fun part, convincing the hubby that something is wrong. He kinda poopoo’s what I am doing. He was off playing D and D last night when I did more testing so I sent him a video on body voltage to watch and said that I am very concerned about the testing that I did. He can’t deny that there is something going on when I see about replicating the testing I did for him. It might wait until Tuesday when we have the carpet replaced. I will be curious to see if it is just the carpeting, which would really really suck since we are keeping carpeting in the living room. We also had an outlet that just quit working a couple of years ago and we cannot figure out why. Maybe that is the source, just not sure.

Now I know why I feel better sitting outside!!!!

### nicld — 2015-06-19T15:56:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-168932>

I have an old old house. I think we saw the first easement on the property was in 1929. My "basement" is actually the old root cellar as the walls in that section are at least 8" think on each side.

I know I do not have a grounding rod since the power coming into the house is already buried from the electrical pole to the house.

After I do a little more testing this weekend (and take more pictures), I will be scheduling a time for an electrician to come out. One of the one that responded does have some knowledge about it but I will make sure that I do more reading this weekend and relisten to the EMF bootcamp again to make sure that I have as much knowledge as I can.

I just tried to stay out of the living room last night and when I was in it, I was grounded. I know that is not the best and that I need to get rid of the source of the high EMF's but that may take a little time so I will keep grounding myself until it gets fixed.

### nicld — 2015-06-21T00:14:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-169093>

Oh holy hell, almost none of my outlets in the house are grounded. One of the few ones is in the bathroom, which we just remodeled and our contractor ran new wires. Since this one is grounded I am thinking that my box is grounded but none of the wiring is. We have been in this house for almost 18 years and we had always talked about getting the wiring redone, guess we should have.

So for now, I am going to keep my grounding strap on as much as I can at home and keep it on the DD. I will be letting the DH know when he gets home that we will be needing to rewire and show him that none of the "three" prong outlets are actually grounded.

It took me a bit but glad I never gave up on rechecking the house and good thing I never bought anything to "plug" in to be grounded. I would have been just zapping myself.

### nicld — 2015-06-30T17:10:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-170088>

So, still doing more testing and rearranging to get the nnEMF’s better in the house. The DH was working OT last night so I took the opportunity to do more testing and mainly in the bedroom. When I would test using the body voltage meter on my Magnetico bed I was still getting higher readings that I was like (2V), way too high. It was even higher on the DH side. I bought an amplifier to make a “buzz stick” to help pinpoint more stuff to look at. I have a sound machine that was plugged into an extension cord coming from the other side of the room but the plug in box was about 2 feet away. BINGO, that plug in went through the roof on the buzz stick. Since I am not ready to give up the sound machine it got moved 5 feet away and on the dresser and not the nightstand. My readings on the meter went down to 0.3 to 0.5V, way better. At these levels I feel it is ok to hook myself up to the grounding rod again at night.

Did some work on the DH’s side as well. We had a large extension cord that was running under his head to the window fan and his alarm clock (which I have now moved as far away from him as I can and inside his dresser drawer). Also rerouted the extension cord to come from the other wall so there is nothing plugged in on the wall where our bed is. I did see what difference I got from shutting off the breakers and did not see a real difference. It might not drop to nothing without shutting off the whole house and that is not going to happen. The readings from his side dropped to below 1V which is good. I will be trying to relisten to week 1 of the EMF webinar again.

### nicld — 2015-06-30T17:15:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-170089>

Next task is getting the electrician to come out and rewire the living room. They were not sure if they could rewire the whole house or if that was even needed. I told him yesterday that the living room HAS to be rewired, that is going to be a non-negotiable task. The readings in that room are so high and my DD and DH spends most of their time in there that I just cannot have readings that high. We have a nice combo of newer ungrounded wiring and the old cloth covered wires. We were hoping to have all of the cloth covered redone but if adding the GFI to the breaker box for the bedrooms helps then I can hold off since the electrician did not sound that happy to do that and the costs that come with it. t was running under his head to the window fan and his alarm clock (which I have now moved as far away from him as I can and inside his dresser drawer). Also rerouted the extension cord to come from the other wall so there is nothing plugged in on the wall where our bed is. I did see what difference I got from shutting off the breakers and did not see a real difference. It might not drop to nothing without shutting off the whole house and that is not going to happen. ### nicld — 2015-07-01T16:15:14+0300

Received the quote yesterday and he tried not to scare me about the cost but worst case scenario is $4700 (gulp) but I kinda expected that. Hoping it will be alot less since for the rewiring of the living room they can go through the basement.

### nicld — 2015-07-01T17:37:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-170228>

Hope I am doing this right but I made my own grounding cord that plugs into the wall to test myself at work. Just have the ground cord attached to the plug and attached to my wire which I have attached to my meter. This is plugged into the wall since I have no way of attaching a wire to a grounding rod outside at work.

I work in an engineering wing, on the ground floor on a painted concrete slab that was added when my company bought the building and has all new electrical and is up to code. When I tested at my office space it looks GREAT (better than the house). With shoes on, my reading was about 0.32 V, when I took my shoes off and put my bare feet on the painted concrete, it dropped to 0.002V. WHOOOHOOOO, I can be grounded at work. I then went and touched my phone not is airplane mode and my salt lamp and it went up to about 1.5V. I then took my bare foot and placed it over by the surge protector and it went to 2.3-3V. I

I am so glad to know that where I spend 45 hours a week is not horrible. Now to just get the BluTech glasses to help protect against the blue light and I should be mitigating all the risks the best I can while still working. I do have a wired keyboard and wired mouse and my monitors are about 2 feet away. Now I will be trying to sit barefoot as much as I can at work. There was no way that I was going to try grounding at work using the electrical system and this just shows it was a good decision and not needed.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hugh-jackman-skin-cancer.12018/post-145460>

Hi @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) and all.
Large debates and discussions going on here in Aussieland on many a facebook and internet sites regarding Hugh Jackman and his latest skin cancer diagnosis.
What frustrates me is that there are still so many Australians that go on and on about how we 'should' use sunscreen due to our ozone layer and sun affect. But they just don't seem to get not just the fact re the chemical damage of sunscreens but the whole 'doughnut hole' affect that you write about.
There is just so much more to this...............
When will so many Australians learn? I reckon when we have Jack Kruse come on down....................

### AJT — 2014-11-01T18:16:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hugh-jackman-skin-cancer.12018/post-145867>

the diagnostic criteria have changed, whereby quasi malignant lesions are now diagnosed as cancer, and on top of that a very large amount of people get skin cancer from indoor light and UVA exposure through windows without the UVB which signals apoptosis of malignant cells via vitamin d synthesis. So going inside can give you cancer.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19519827](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19519827)

This is from Medical Hypothesis:

"We hypothesize that one factor involves indoor exposures to UVA (321–400nm) passing through windows, which can cause mutations and can break down vitamin D3 formed after outdoor UVB (290–320nm) exposure, and the other factor involves low levels of cutaneous vitamin D3.

After vitamin D3 forms, melanoma cells can convert it to the hormone, 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D3, or calcitriol, which causes growth inhibition and apoptotic cell death in vitro and in vivo.

… We agree that intense, intermittent outdoor UV overexposures and sunburns initiate CMM \[cutaneous malignant melanoma\]; we now propose that increased UVA exposures and inadequately maintained cutaneous levels of vitamin D3 promotes CMM."

# Thread: Hacking sleep timing and patterns
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-sleep-timing-and-patterns.12022/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sjoshua — 2014-10-30T01:32:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-sleep-timing-and-patterns.12022/post-145602>

Before humans started to make our own light, it seems that the evidence shows it was most common that humans practiced segmented sleeping, which is sleeping with a period of wakefulness in the middle of it for a couple hours. Please check out this TED talk about it (~4 minutes): [http://www.ted.com/talks/jessa\_gamble\_how\_to\_sleep?language=en](http://www.ted.com/talks/jessa_gamble_how_to_sleep?language=en)

I'm of the understanding that having a well-functioning circadian rhythm is vital to achieving and maintaining health. Following the sun's schedule seems to be the easiest way to do this on Earth...

But, what of taking the reins and re-programming your body to it's own circadian rhythm? I'm referring to polyphasic sleep, which in essence is poly- or, multiple, phased sleep patterns. The idea put simply is that the beneficial sections/stages of our sleep are intermingled with light-ish, unnecessary segments when sleeping monophasically, and by training your body and brain to a different schedule you can increase usable awake/alert time by learning to get into the beneficial stages of sleep better and faster while reducing the unnecessary light-ish stages of sleep. (ample info here for those who want to learn further: [http://www.polyphasicsociety.com/polyphasic-sleep/beginners/](http://www.polyphasicsociety.com/polyphasic-sleep/beginners/))

On the most extreme schedule termed 'Uberman', there are 6, 20-minute naps spaced exactly 4 hours apart throughout the day, totaling 2 hours of total sleep time per 24-hour period. The scheduling of said naps and proper timing would be paramount to entraining a new circadian rhythm, so of course it wouldn't be overly convenient for most (i.e. desk job folk), but there are a slew of other templates that even work for 9-5ers that can reduce total daily sleep time to 3-7 hours.

By the numbers, it is an appealing choice - quick math tells you how much you could truly gain here:
'Average' sleeper:
Per night: 8 hours
Extrapolated per year (8\*365 days): 2,920 hours
2,920 hours = ~122 days/year spent sleeping

Polyphasic sleeper:
(the absolute floor minimum polyphasic schedule does 2 hours sleep/24 hours; for a more reasonable schedule, let's estimate we cut sleep down to a conservative\* 5 hours/day)
Per night: 5 hours
Extrapolated per year (5\*365 days): 1,825 hours
1,825 hours = ~76 days/year sleeping

\*conservative for poly- schedules

122 - 76 = +46 days per year of realized awake time. Basically an extra 21 hours every week...

This means, for every 8 years that pass, you would realize the equivalent of an extra 365 days of life awake! In an 80 year 'lifetime', that's an extra 10 years...

I'm sure this wouldn't appeal to everyone.. Also, numbers are a human fallacy, so I try to think much beyond them. Also, I don't think it would be wise to even approach or consider this unless already in a healthy state!

I'm curious if anyone here has ever experimented with such a thing? Thoughts? Contraindications?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-30T02:26:31+0200

### Da-mo — 2014-10-31T01:46:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-sleep-timing-and-patterns.12022/post-145706>

Even though as a shiftworker I would love this to be possible I'm thinking of how Jack described various cycles in the last Q&A webinar . . .

Picturing each cycle as a juggler and theres a number of jugglers and then suddenly they start sharing the balls they are juggling . . . when you mess with the timing of one it necessitates either affecting the timing of them all or else they stop sharing the balls.

Still, being that we are meant to be metastable and adaptable maybe it is possible that mutliple cycles could adapt together in a way different to that described in CT7 [http://jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-7/](http://jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-7/)

CT7 also contains the Jessica Gamble Tedx

I just remembered a guy I studied with who was invovlved in an experiment/test as an armourer in the RAF. Basically they were worked into sleep deprivation while uploading and downloading armaments as part of an exercise and monitored for mistakes. During debreif they found out that while they continued to function acceptably in their tasks, they became increasingly unresponsive to anything anyone said to them. So perhaps training to increase the priority of one function means that other functions may be dropped, de-emphasized or damaged as resources dwindle.

I believe there is someone on this forum who works in the feild of sleep but sorry cant remeber who it was.

### Da-mo — 2014-10-31T02:08:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-sleep-timing-and-patterns.12022/post-145710>

My sister is a flight attendant and some of her training regarding sleep was different to mine so I tried it and found it useful.

She was taught that any sleep should be less than 40 minutes or longer than 2 hours and anything beyond 2 hours should be in multiples of 2 hours.

My n=1 . . . . .

I have tested this over the years and found that it has a large effect on whther you feel rested when waking. Anything between 40 minutes and 2 hours made me feel worse than if I hadnt slept at all. I'm sure this has some link to cycles of brainwaves or stages of sleep but not sure what it is exactly. Less than 40 minutes was ok and more than 2 hours even better - didnt seem to make much difference which part of the day or night this occured.

### sjoshua — 2014-10-31T04:19:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-sleep-timing-and-patterns.12022/post-145724>

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=145710)
>
> My sister is a flight attendant and some of her training regarding sleep was different to mine so I tried it and found it useful.
>
> She was taught that any sleep should be less than 40 minutes or longer than 2 hours and anything beyond 2 hours should be in multiples of 2 hours.
>
> My n=1 . . . . .
>
> I have tested this over the years and found that it has a large effect on whther you feel rested when waking. Anything between 40 minutes and 2 hours made me feel worse than if I hadnt slept at all. I'm sure this has some link to cycles of brainwaves or stages of sleep but not sure what it is exactly. Less than 40 minutes was ok and more than 2 hours even better - didnt seem to make much difference which part of the day or night this occured.

That's interesting, it aligns with some of the data I am finding on polyphasic sleep. I used to stay up late and take afternoon naps in college, ala a schedule that would be considered segmented or maybe moreso siesta, but irregularly implemented. Looking back, multiples of 90 minutes worked best for me for naps.

Sadly we are mostly stuck with N=1 data here, since similar to long-term keto dieting there is no solid research to reference... We are swimming in unknown waters taking cues from other swimmers who may be just as lost for all we know  But someone's gotta take the first swim.

Curious @[Da-mo](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9712/) : Do you have any idea if the guy you'd studied with was in a short term vs long term study? I.e. were they given ~3-4 weeks for their body to at least begin to adjust to a differing schedule, or merely thrown into sleep deprivation? I ask because in the information I can find, it seems there is a cross-over/transition phase (similar to 'carb flu' or whatever you'd term it for keto) that takes a week to a month to en-train your system to operate on the new schedule during which 'sleep deprivation' symptoms are expected. After that point though, the schedule apparently becomes normal and the reduced sleep time would no longer mean being in a sleep deprived state, rather operating at full/normal capacity (or potentially beyond; some uberman schedulers seem to even get a feeling of added/super energy vs. their prior monophasic schedules).

### Da-mo — 2014-10-31T08:06:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-sleep-timing-and-patterns.12022/post-145750>

Having done shiftwork for 20+ years, I am of the opinion that you never get used to it - you just get used to feeling like crap so it feels normal - then years into it you start getting brain fog etc. My mental abilities are nowhere near where they were 10 years ago.

What makes you realise is when you go off shift and return to normal sleep patterns - around 2 weeks you suddenly have this massive increase in energy and feel really good. Thats when you realise how not normal the new normal was.

No prep, they were thrown into it as a drill but they didnt know it was a drill - scenario was a submarine appearing and disappearing within maritime limits - submarine detected, upload weapons, submarine disappears, download weapons. I think the RAF was just testing how far they could push their people before they made a potentially fatal mistake

### sjoshua — 2014-10-31T20:03:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-sleep-timing-and-patterns.12022/post-145810>

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=145750)
>
> Having done shiftwork for 20+ years, I am of the opinion that you never get used to it - you just get used to feeling like crap so it feels normal - then years into it you start getting brain fog etc. My mental abilities are nowhere near where they were 10 years ago.
>
> What makes you realise is when you go off shift and return to normal sleep patterns - around 2 weeks you suddenly have this massive increase in energy and feel really good. Thats when you realise how not normal the new normal was.
>
> No prep, they were thrown into it as a drill but they didnt know it was a drill - scenario was a submarine appearing and disappearing within maritime limits - submarine detected, upload weapons, submarine disappears, download weapons. I think the RAF was just testing how far they could push their people before they made a potentially fatal mistake

Interesting, I appreciate the perspective, Da-mo. I can understand how those type of experiments would probably be just throwing people into it, since most do not practice outside of monophasic sleep so that would be the truer 'test' of their abilities given an emergency arises (since, obviously with an emergency there isn't a month of lead time to adapt to a new schedule )

To clarify, in your own experience, by shift work do you mean constantly entertaining a different sleep schedule: i.e. working nights, then days, then nights over consecutive days/weeks? Or, do you mean long-term night shifts, for example. I ask because, at least conceptually, I think the difference there would be night and day vs polyphasic sleep. If you are switching back and forth, or constantly moving your sleep timing around, you are never actually giving your body a chance to adapt to a new set schedule, so I'd expect it to be very difficult or impossible to ever get used to. Where as polyphasic sleep emphasizes the importance of being very strict to your newly established/establishing schedule; it does not entail a rotating or shifting rhythm.

But of importance here: polyphasic sleepers seem to suffer sleep deprived symptoms **IF** they go off their established schedule, the same as someone would missing a monophasic night of good sleep, or alternatively I could imagine same as someone working swing shifts that cannot get into a regular cycle.

### Da-mo — 2014-10-31T22:27:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-sleep-timing-and-patterns.12022/post-145824>

12 hour shifts . . . . night, night, off, day, day, off, off, off . . . . and then it starts again every 8 days

I think we turn around twice as much as we need too - Id rather 4 nights then 4 off then 4 days then 4 off if I have to do nightshifts at all.

I do try to keep a tally of total hours slept but its obviously not the same as normal sleep.

Im working towards not doing shiftwork anymore - its still at least a year away. This is my personal thunderstorm - shiftwork working in a power station.

Hows that for a perfect storm?

The stuff I have learned here has had very many positive effects so far - especially the diet and CT and hacking most of my non-work and some of my work artificial light and EMF out of my life. But I know optimal wont come until I've hacked the storm.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-sleep-timing-and-patterns.12022/post-146236>

> [kovita said:](/goto/post?id=146041)
>
> Joshua, form my experience it does not have to be necessarily proper mediation. I am not trained in meditating at all and i understand it is not easy. I do often some sort of my meditation. I just lay down with my eyes closed and concentrate on a good and healthy feelings traveling through my body. I often thing about myself as if I was a perfectly harmonized musical instrument and I let the harmony vibrate through me. I find it much more refreshing than a nap and after 20-30min Ia m as good as a new one ;-)

Those sounds like very effective meditation strategies  Thank you for your insights here, I think it goes to support the idea that a POTENTIAL replacement for a polyphasic 'nap' would be an equal-length meditation, as long as the individual is adept in entering the proper brainwave frequencies.

### Da-mo — 2014-11-10T04:19:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-sleep-timing-and-patterns.12022/post-146705>

So I've just done 16, 20 and 30 mins in an icey tub after dinner for the last 3 evenings during my days off.

I can maintain CT at this time of the day for 6 days out of every 8.

For the other 2 days I work 2 x 12hour nightshifts in a high EMF and artificial light environment from 7pm to 7am.

I usually wear the ice-vest during work for about 2 hours out of the 12 hour shift near the beginning and end of the shift.

I have the impression that the best time to CT is after exercise, dinner and before sleep to help maximally re-condense matter while we sleep. This also is a time when cortisol should be lower as melatonin starts kicking in.
I'm wondering whether it is worth the risk to CT before, between and/or after the nightshifts and what, if any, the best timing would be. My main concern is the heart risk associated with doing CT when cortisol is high like when we wake up naturally at sunrise as mentioned here . . . .

> When you rise what happens? [Cortisol spikes in the AM.](http://jackkruse.com/primal-cortisol-response/) What does cortisol do? It makes collagen swell because it unzips part of it by removing electrons from it. What does this do? It cause cells to swell because they become more loose in their tensegrity system and lose their electric and magnetic charge. This process is usually self-limited by your own redox state, but it if is low to begin with it can lead to more serious medical problems over time. This is why more heart attacks occur in the AM than any other time in the day as well. [http://jackkruse.com/tensegrity-2-cortisol/](http://jackkruse.com/tensegrity-2-cortisol/)

It goes wthout saying that the nightshifts are the problem here - Im moving away from them but its still 6 months to a year before any significant change in work hours will happen. Until then I'm wanting to do what I can to minimise the damage. I do not have any heart problems that Im aware of and Id like to keep it that way.

Should I CT before the nightshift (when my body should be thinking about sleep), after the nightshift (when my body should be waking up but needs to go to sleep) or skip CT (except for the ice-vest which has not been a problem thius far) until I'm back to normal sleep hours?

Hmmm, thinking about the question a little more Im beginning to think the jetlag Rx might be more beneficial . . . .

### DavidL — 2015-03-24T03:57:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-sleep-timing-and-patterns.12022/post-159335>

Not sure if you guys have ScribD, but I'm about to dig into this one. I have a strong interest in optimizing shift work. I've done it for 7 years now, but I will soon leave that job to run my business full time. My parting gift to my co-workers will be something about coping with shiftwork.
[https://www.scribd.com/search?query=making+shiftwork+tolerable](https://www.scribd.com/search?query=making+shiftwork+tolerable)

### DavidL — 2015-03-24T04:12:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-sleep-timing-and-patterns.12022/post-159336>

I came off night shift last night and fasted until 6pm. When I woke up I drank a bunch of water, took a cold shower and a bunch of ubiquinol.

### Da-mo — 2015-03-25T23:06:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-sleep-timing-and-patterns.12022/post-159431>

I often do that fast as well - eat at around 5pm before nightshift, come home in the morning and straight to sleep without eating, then eat when I rise sometime in the afternoon - resulting in a fast somewhere between 19 and 24hours.

If I don't do that fast, I might eat at work around 4am for an 11hour fast (sleeping about 8am).

Generally, if I am able to get a few hours sleep at work I'll do the long fast - if not then I'll eat at 4am (nightshifts shift are 12hrs 7pm-7am).

I find using blue blockers from a few hours before the end of the shift until bedtime helps significantly.

It still seems that we do what we can - but it still comes down to trying to rebuild our reserves durring the non-nightshift times.

BTW, I find it difficult to get enough fat in my diet and as such it is predominantly protein. Since Dr. Kruse had said that intermittent fasting turns off mTOR I am considering the IF a good thing in my case. I also wear an ice vest for an hour at the beginning and end of every shift.

> The primary reason their system works is their insurance will pay for preventive care - herbs, exercise, accupuncture, ect... Great idea to prevent disease then fix a huge hole in a dam with your finger lol

If their docs are trained here, it means the medicine is similar....paying for preventive care is good economics, but does not equal good preventive care.....I would say that Chinese society when it is intact has a fairly good preventive system built into the lifestyle....Taiwan has many good traditional doctors as they escaped the "standardization" of Chinese Medicine by Mao....

### billy — 2014-11-03T04:20:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/farm-raised-shrimp-from-abroad-sold-as-wild-beware.12029/post-145998>

> [BTA said:](/goto/post?id=145735)
>
> Taiwan has the best healthcare in the world. Pretty interesting story in how they did it.

> Big Pharma has their hands in doctors pockets - the amount of kickbacks are from a few dollars to millions $$..... Just one example of what your doc makes in extra money - see website. Just type in your doc and their name will come up:
>
> [http://projects.propublica.org/docdollars/](http://projects.propublica.org/docdollars/)
> .
> .

I like the hospitals and Docs in Thailand. I especially like that they are not 100% trained here, by big pharma.
Bumrungrad, BNH ... 2 of my favorite hospitals there, my whole family has used them and loves it there. We got spoiled fast and it makes our system look dysfunctional when we get home.

Two gyno's here wanted to do a full hysterectomy on my wife who is Thai. She doesn't trust the medical system here so we went to Thailand to put her at ease. It was just a POLYP! That's it. We went as walk-ins into BNH in the afternoon and it was Dx'd and done by evening. $1,875 out the door - private recovery suite w/ shower and guest bed included. The Dr spoke great English and showed me the ultrasound before the procedure to reassure me. Her pain has been gone for years and she checks out fine.

We got an MRI on a walk-in there for about $310 US retail tourist. They apologized and said we'd have to wait... 45 minutes! lol
And the seafood ain't bad there either.

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-10-30T23:19:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bluetooth-toothbrush.12030/post-145688>

how can it be that they had it sorted when they were 5 years old and all of a sudden a machine has to tell them...

smart appliance/dumbpeople

### sjoshua — 2014-10-30T23:38:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bluetooth-toothbrush.12030/post-145690>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=145688)
>
> how can it be that they had it sorted when they were 5 years old and all of a sudden a machine has to tell them...
>
> smart appliance/dumbpeople

It comes down to humans wanting to be told what to do instead of creating the thought themselves... there is less effort involved if you only have to stop when told vs tracking how long you've been brushing and making a decision. Obviously, people could argue they are half asleep when brushing their teeth in the morning anyways, but it seems that it doesn't end with the simple things...

Stuff like this is interestingly humiliating for our species: [http://newsoffice.mit.edu/2014/want-happy-worker-let-robots-take-control](http://newsoffice.mit.edu/2014/want-happy-worker-let-robots-take-control)

### yewwei.tan — 2014-10-31T01:04:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bluetooth-toothbrush.12030/post-145703>

Hahaha, now look what Silicon VCs are funding -- [http://www.bothsidesofthetable.com/...ous-plan-to-make-electricity-as-easy-as-wifi/](http://www.bothsidesofthetable.com/2014/10/30/the-audacious-plan-to-make-electricity-as-easy-as-wifi/)

That's Mark Suster talking about uBeam -- [http://ubeam.com/](http://ubeam.com/) . It's an attempt to make Wireless Electricity for wireless charging of phones, wearable devices, etc ...

But as much as I'd like to mock that, the underlying technology, Ultrasonic Transduction, is attempting to do the transmission via sound waves. I don't know what that means, and I don't know the nnEMF implications. It'll probably kill you faster today, but maybe it's the inklings of a first design that allows us to turn nnEMF into harmless forms of energy ...

# Thread: David's Journal - Finally Aligned
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/davids-journal-finally-aligned.12031/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 191
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DavidL — 2014-10-30T23:27:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/davids-journal-finally-aligned.12031/post-145689>

Hello, here is my journal. I'm a new gold member.

It's long but when you know my history, you'll see the many reasons why I am here. (Or scroll to the bottom to learn why I'm here)

I am a Canadian/Saskatchewan prairie kid born and raised. My ancestors are non-native to Canada. Icelandic/Swede/English descendant. We've been here for 80 years (makes me third gen'r in Canada.) Saskatchewan was isolated and industrialized except for farming until recently.

The Icelanders hung with the Icelanders. The Ukrainians with the Ukrainians. etc. etc.
My diet came from mom's side (Icelandic). They were excellent gardeners. My early life was filled with tons of outdoor activity, always playing hockey and riding a bike. **We ate seasonal, garden grown food, canned most foods for winter.**

Nintendo didn't make it's way into our household until 1987. After that our health went south. We were caught up in the low fat craze, I ate chips and pop like it was the only thing around. My diet is linked to my poor performance in school but the sugar fueled me in sports. I was a good athlete, could compete in every sport but I was aggressive (penalties). In school I was smart and would treat tests like a competition. But I couldn't stay focused for long. Immediate disinterest and onto the next thing.
**(Good circadian match until age 7)**

Became intitally interested in health around the age of 19 (1999) after realizing I was in horrible shape. I got on the **Body-for-Life** train with decent results. I swam in a cold pool everyday during this time. (Most ripped I'd ever been....and now I know why)

Performed poorly in my first two years of university due to an inability to focus for long periods of time. Fortunately, in 2001 I was acquainted with a group of people pushing Hemp Hearts...we ate it everyday, tons of it. The increase in fat improved my ability to think for longer periods of time. **Hemp, organic foods and swimming saved my academic career. (Business/Econ)**

From 2004-2007 I worked long hours in a high stress political job under several government ministers including the Agriculture Minister and Premier. I was good at it and I enjoyed meeting interesting people.

Sidenote:
The government I worked for is the party of [Tommy Douglas,](http://www.thecanadianencyclopedia.ca/en/article/tommy-douglas/) otherwise known as the father of socialized medicine. Tommy _Douglas created the first ever government-funded health insurance program in 1947 in Saskatchewan._
**(naturally I have a strong interest in public health policy)**

_Right now In Canada, people would rather debate the merits of Public/Private than to debate how to improve health care. I want to change that....We need teach people how to practice private personal health care._

_I want to contribute to a new vision/plan for the future of healthcare. I believe that the Jack Kruse community can help._

My diet during 2004-2008 was a standard mix of proteins, carbs, fats, vegetables, and fruit. I believed protein was for muscles, carbs gave you energy, and vegetables would stop disease. Most of my meals were from hotels, convention centers and restaurants. However, I **consumed alcohol in excess** after hours and weekends. I stayed in shape working out, ultimate frisbee, snowboarding, and longboarding.

In 2007 I lost my job (the party I was involved with lost power), and found myself on severance. Depressed and anxious (just bought a house), I took the first job I could at an oil refinery where my father was employed for 40 years.

The money was good. Shift work. Two days, two nights, five off. In 2009 **reality struck while working in the de-coking unit as a process operator**...the coker is hell on earth folks......I cannot describe it any other way.....this is the bottom of the bottoms....where you remove the metals and particulates left over from fractionated oil....heavy metal dust everywhere, H2S, sour water, 100 million BTU/hour heater, pumps...

**REALITY: It became extremely apparent in 2009 that I was living in a toxic world.**

Health had to become my number 1 priority, or I would die here. I began to plan a different future.

Watched Food Matters in 2009. Immediately bought a juicer. Began to learn about supplements. Bought books by David Wolfe and discovered raw food juice youtube guy [Dan McDonald.](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XLTkZJTTUq4&index=2&list=PLAF3BE29C0D1D66E7)

I paid attention to getting grounded, and bought a grounding kit. My diet was a mixing of paleo and vegan.

After Food Matters, I was instantly intrigued with Wolfe and superfoods in particular. Began to feel really good on short bouts of raw food and juices and fasting. My goal was to detox the metals and feel good all the time.

**Most importantly, I believed at this point that every single disease could be prevented or reversed.**

Chlorella, spirulina, cilantro, carrot, apple and parsley juices became a staple. When I ate meat it was mostly wild game. (This is hunting country)

In 2012 I started a company based on my lifestyle and what I was doing to feel better.

In 2013 I **quit alcohol** realizing that my business has potential. I got into Dave Asprey and Jack Kruse after hearing their Bulletproof podcast. I was searching for the next level in my own health. I was looking to go beyond dogma. I wanted to find the ultimate lifestyle.

Jack Kruse grabbed my attention with the words **"Quantum Biology".** I've been interested in the soul, in metaphysics/quantum theory...watched many hours of David Wilcock and Nassim Harramein.

To apply QED to biology? Wow, I thought, I need to listen to this. This Jack guy might be on to something...he might have some answers....

BOOM. My mind is blown. Every post is fascinating. I am learning a lot and really loving it.
This is fun.

After just a few weeks of applying the principles of this community, I am experiencing a greater level of health. My body and mind have responded extremely well.

**GOALS:**
\- to continually experience greater levels of health
\- Learn and understand the science
\- Be able to read geek
\- Help others with this knowledge
\- reverse any conditions I may be experiencing
\- Influence public health policy
\- understand the causes of disease
\- find the most effective path to health
\- discover why I see so much disease around me and to do something about

**Current Health Issues:**
\- Sciatic pain at times
\- modern technological lifestyle

I'm just trying to do the best I can. Life is a journey. I love the journey.

### nonchalant — 2014-10-31T00:45:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/davids-journal-finally-aligned.12031/post-145699>

Welcome David! Do you get much seafood with your Icelandic background? I guess sunshine is getting rare there already. Do you mind getting cold?

### DavidL — 2014-10-31T02:37:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/davids-journal-finally-aligned.12031/post-145714>

Thanks for the welcome. I do eat a fair bit of seafood now, more than ever. Red meat and pork is the staple here on the prairies.

We knew the farmers personally who raised the animals. No one had much money so livestock was always grass-fed.

Switching to fish almost daily made me feel really good, better than expected. My relatives in Reykjavik eat fish everyday.

Longevity runs in the family...for now. Great-grandmother was 99. Grandmother made it to 97. Grandfather carried his golf clubs well into his 80's.
Fibromyalgia is what I was told he had. We ate a lot of carbs in winter.

I am used to the cold. In fact I love it (Snowboarder). Every night as kids at a cold rink or tobogganing.
In high school we rarely wore jackets even at -20\*C. Snowstorms will dump up to several feet of snow at a time.

By default we are always shoveling snow in the cold and dark....I used to hate snowstorms....but am looking forward to them now.
Days are getting short, dark by 6 pm now.

One objective is to learn how to properly handle these cold dark days north of the 49th Parallel.

Limiting light after dark is not how we live here...lots of time indoors with the TV on watching hockey.
